+# йЎәи—Өж‘ёз“ңејҸжҗңзҙў
+
+## е®ҡд№ү
+
+еңЁиҝҷеә§зҹҘиҜҶеә“йҮҢпјҢ`йЎәи—Өж‘ёз“ңејҸжҗңзҙў` жҢҮзҡ„жҳҜдёҚд»Һзӣ®ж Үзӯ”жЎҲжң¬иә«зӣҙжҺҘжҗңиө·пјҢиҖҢжҳҜе…ҲжүҫеҲ°дёҺзӣ®ж Үй«ҳеәҰзӣёе…ізҡ„еӨ–еӣҙзәҝзҙўпјҢеҶҚйЎәзқҖиҝҷдәӣзәҝзҙўдёҖжӯҘжӯҘзј©е°ҸиҢғеӣҙгҖҒйҖјиҝ‘зӣ®ж ҮгҖӮ
+
+## дёәд»Җд№ҲйҮҚиҰҒ
+
+еҫҲеӨҡдёңиҘҝд№ӢжүҖд»ҘйҡҫжүҫпјҢдёҚжҳҜеӣ дёәе®Ңе…ЁжІЎжңүе…¬ејҖз—•иҝ№пјҢиҖҢжҳҜеӣ дёәе®ғд»¬дёҚдјҡз”ЁжңҖзӣҙзҷҪзҡ„е…ій”®иҜҚжҡҙйңІиҮӘе·ұгҖӮиҝҷж—¶еҰӮжһңиҝҳеҸӘйқ вҖңзӣ®ж ҮиҜҚзӣҙжҗңвҖқпјҢжҗңзҙўж•ҲжһңеҫҖеҫҖеҫҲе·®гҖӮйЎәи—Өж‘ёз“ңејҸжҗңзҙўзҡ„д»·еҖје°ұеңЁдәҺпјҡе…ҲжүҫвҖңдјҡз•ҷдёӢз—•иҝ№зҡ„дәәгҖҒең°ж–№гҖҒе…іиҒ”зү©вҖқпјҢеҶҚеҸҚжҺЁзӣ®ж ҮгҖӮ
+
+## е…ій”®иҰҒзӮ№
+
+- е…ҲжүҫзәҝзҙўиҠӮзӮ№пјҢиҖҢдёҚжҳҜе…ҲжүҫжңҖз»Ҳзӯ”жЎҲгҖӮ
+- зәҝзҙўиҠӮзӮ№еҸҜд»ҘжҳҜе№ҝе‘ҠдҪҚгҖҒе”®еҗҺзҫӨгҖҒдёӘдәәдё»йЎөгҖҒеҗҢзұ»з«ҷзӮ№гҖҒи®Ёи®әеҢәгҖҒе…ій”®иҜҚз»„еҗҲгҖҒеҹҹеҗҚзүҮж®өгҖӮ
+- жҗңзҙўеј•ж“Һзҡ„дҪңз”ЁдёҚеҸӘжҳҜжүҫзҪ‘йЎөпјҢиҝҳиғҪеё®еҠ©дҪ йӘҢиҜҒвҖңе“ӘдәӣеҹҹеҗҚгҖҒе…ій”®иҜҚгҖҒеңәжҷҜеҪјжӯӨејәе…іиҒ”вҖқгҖӮ
+- `site:` иҝҷзұ»жҗңзҙўиҜӯжі•зҡ„д»·еҖјпјҢеңЁдәҺжҠҠжҗңзҙўиҢғеӣҙд»Һж•ҙдёӘдә’иҒ”зҪ‘зј©еҲ°жҹҗдёӘе·ІзҹҘз«ҷзӮ№жҲ–жҹҗзұ»з«ҷзӮ№гҖӮ
+- зңҹжӯЈжңүж•Ҳзҡ„йғЁеҲҶеҫҖеҫҖдёҚжҳҜжҹҗдёӘеӣәе®ҡй“ҫжҺҘпјҢиҖҢжҳҜвҖңеҰӮдҪ•жһ„йҖ дёӢдёҖиҪ®жҹҘиҜўвҖқгҖӮ
+
+## иҫ№з•Ң
+
+- е®ғдёҚзӯүдәҺвҖңжҗңеҫ—жӣҙж·ұвҖқжҲ–вҖңжҗңеҫ—жӣҙд№…вҖқгҖӮ
+- е®ғд№ҹдёҚзӯүдәҺзҒ°иүІжё йҒ“жҗңзҙўжң¬иә«пјӣзҒ°иүІжё йҒ“еҸӘжҳҜиҝҷз§Қж–№жі•зҡ„дёҖдёӘдҪҝз”ЁеңәжҷҜгҖӮ
+- иҝҷз§Қж–№жі•еҸҜд»Ҙз”ЁдәҺжүҫиө„ж–ҷгҖҒжүҫзӨҫеҢәи®Ёи®әгҖҒжүҫжЎҲдҫӢгҖҒжүҫеҺҶеҸІйЎөйқўпјҢдҪҶе…·дҪ“дҪҝз”Ёд»ҚиҰҒиҖғиҷ‘еҗҲи§„гҖҒеҸҜйқ жҖ§е’ҢйЈҺйҷ©гҖӮ
+
+## еёёи§ҒеҲҶжӯ§
+
+- дёҖз§ҚеҒҡжі•еҒҸеҗ‘зӣҙжҺҘе…ій”®иҜҚжЈҖзҙўпјҢдјҳзӮ№жҳҜеҝ«пјҢзјәзӮ№жҳҜе®№жҳ“еҚЎеңЁиЎЁеұӮз»“жһңгҖӮ
+- еҸҰдёҖз§ҚеҒҡжі•еҒҸеҗ‘е…Ҳе»әзәҝзҙўзҪ‘з»ңпјҢеҶҚйҖҗжӯҘзј©е°ҸиҢғеӣҙпјҢдјҳзӮ№жҳҜжӣҙе®№жҳ“ж‘ёеҲ°йҡҗи—Ҹз»“жһ„пјҢзјәзӮ№жҳҜжӣҙдҫқиө–еҲӨж–ӯеҠӣгҖӮ
+- еңЁдҝЎжҒҜзЁҖз–ҸгҖҒе…ій”®иҜҚж··д№ұгҖҒз»“жһңй«ҳеәҰдёӘжҖ§еҢ–зҡ„еңәжҷҜдёӢпјҢеҗҺиҖ…йҖҡеёёжӣҙжңүж•ҲгҖӮ
+

@@

-- её–еӯҗдёҚжҳҜзӣҙжҺҘиҙҙеӣәе®ҡеә—й“әй“ҫжҺҘпјҢиҖҢжҳҜеңЁи®ІдёҖз§ҚвҖңйЎәи—Өж‘ёз“ңвҖқзҡ„жүҫжё йҒ“ж–№жі•гҖӮ
+- жқҘжәҗжҳҜ linux.do дёҠзҡ„дёҖзҜҮзҰҸеҲ©зҫҠжҜӣеё–пјҢдҪңиҖ…дёә `revival`пјҢж—¶й—ҙжҳҜ 2026-04-15гҖӮ
+- её–еӯҗдёҚжҳҜзӣҙжҺҘиҙҙеӣәе®ҡеә—й“әй“ҫжҺҘпјҢиҖҢжҳҜеңЁи®ІдёҖз§ҚвҖңйЎәи—Өж‘ёз“ңвҖқзҡ„жҗңзҙўж–№жі•гҖӮ
 - дҪңиҖ…жҠҠзӣ®ж Үжё йҒ“еҲҶжҲҗдёӨзұ»пјҡTG еҸ·е•Ҷе’ҢеҸ‘еҚЎе№іеҸ°пјҢе№¶е»әи®®йҖҡиҝҮе№ҝе‘ҠдҪҚгҖҒе”®еҗҺзҫӨгҖҒжҗңзҙўеј•ж“Һзӯүж–№ејҸй—ҙжҺҘеҸ‘зҺ°гҖӮ
 - её–еӯҗиҝҳжҸҗеҲ°дёҖдәӣдҪҺд»·еҢәй—ҙе’ҢиҙЁдҝқиҜҙжі•пјҢдҪҶиҝҷдәӣдҝЎжҒҜжҳҺжҳҫе…·жңүжһҒејәж—¶ж•ҲжҖ§пјҢиҖҢдё”йҡҸж—¶еҸҜиғҪеӨұж•ҲгҖӮ

-- иҝҷзұ»еҶ…е®№жӣҙеғҸзҒ°иүІеёӮеңәж ·жң¬пјҢдёҚйҖӮеҗҲзӣҙжҺҘеҪ“жҲҗзЁіе®ҡзҹҘиҜҶжҲ–жҺЁиҚҗж–№жЎҲгҖӮ
+- иҝҷзұ»еҶ…е®№дҪңдёәжё йҒ“ж ·жң¬жң¬иә«дёҚйҖӮеҗҲеҪ“жҺЁиҚҗж–№жЎҲпјҢдҪҶе…¶дёӯвҖңзәҝзҙўй©ұеҠЁжҗңзҙўвҖқзҡ„йғЁеҲҶеҖјеҫ—еҚ•зӢ¬жҸҗзӮјгҖӮ
@@
-- иҝҷд»ҪжқҘжәҗзҡ„д»·еҖјдё»иҰҒеңЁдәҺи®°еҪ•дёҖз§ҚзҒ°иүІи®ўйҳ…жё йҒ“зҡ„еҸ‘зҺ°ж–№жі•пјҢиҖҢдёҚжҳҜжҸҗдҫӣеҸҜйқ гҖҒзЁіе®ҡгҖҒеҸҜеӨҚз”Ёзҡ„иҙӯд№°з»“и®әгҖӮ
-- е®ғејәи°ғзҡ„жҳҜвҖңжҗңзҙўе’ҢеҸ‘зҺ°и·Ҝеҫ„вҖқпјҢдёҚжҳҜе№іеҸ°е®ҳж–№иғҪеҠӣпјҢд№ҹдёҚжҳҜе…¬ејҖйҖҸжҳҺзҡ„е•Ҷе“ҒдҝЎжҒҜгҖӮ
+- иҝҷд»ҪжқҘжәҗжңҖеҖјеҫ—дҝқз•ҷзҡ„пјҢдёҚжҳҜдҪҺд»·жё йҒ“з»“и®әпјҢиҖҢжҳҜвҖңжҗңзҙўе’ҢеҸ‘зҺ°и·Ҝеҫ„вҖқгҖӮ
+- е®ғејәи°ғзҡ„жҳҜеҰӮдҪ•йҖҡиҝҮзәҝзҙўиҠӮзӮ№йҖҗжӯҘзј©е°ҸиҢғеӣҙпјҢиҖҢдёҚжҳҜдҫқиө–жҹҗдёӘеӣәе®ҡе…ҘеҸЈгҖӮ

-- иҝҷзұ»жқҘжәҗиҝҳеёҰжңүжҳҺжҳҫзҡ„еҗҲи§„гҖҒйЈҺжҺ§е’ҢиҜҲйӘ—йЈҺйҷ©пјҢеӣ жӯӨжӣҙйҖӮеҗҲдҪңдёәзҺ°иұЎи®°еҪ•пјҢиҖҢдёҚжҳҜж–№жі•жІүж·ҖгҖӮ
+- иҝҷзұ»жқҘжәҗиҝҳеёҰжңүжҳҺжҳҫзҡ„еҗҲи§„гҖҒйЈҺжҺ§е’ҢиҜҲйӘ—йЈҺйҷ©пјҢеӣ жӯӨжӣҙйҖӮеҗҲдҪңдёәвҖңж–№жі•ж ·жң¬ + йЈҺйҷ©ж ·жң¬вҖқпјҢиҖҢдёҚжҳҜиҙӯд№°жҢҮеҚ—гҖӮ
@@
 - еҺҹж–ҮжҳҺзЎ®жҠҠзӣ®ж Үж”ҫеңЁ `Plus`гҖҒ`Team` зӯүдҪҺд»·иҺ·еҸ–жё йҒ“дёҠпјҢе№¶жҠҠ `TG еҸ·е•Ҷ` е’Ң `еҚЎзҪ‘` еҪ“жҲҗдё»иҰҒзәҝзҙўжқҘжәҗгҖӮ
 - еҺҹж–ҮжҸҗеҲ°йҖҡиҝҮе№ҝе‘ҠдҪҚгҖҒе”®еҗҺзҫӨе’ҢжҗңзҙўиҜӯжі•зј©е°ҸиҢғеӣҙпјҢдҪҶжІЎжңүжҸҗдҫӣзЁіе®ҡе®ҳж–№е…ҘеҸЈгҖӮ
+- еҺҹж–Үз»ҷеҮәзҡ„зңҹжӯЈеҸҜиҝҒз§»йғЁеҲҶпјҢжҳҜвҖңе…Ҳжүҫдјҡжү“е№ҝе‘Ҡзҡ„дәәе’Ңең°ж–№пјҢеҶҚд»Һе…іиҒ”з«ҷзӮ№е’ҢеҹҹеҗҚйҮҢз»§з»ӯжҗңвҖқзҡ„зәҝзҙўжһ„йҖ жҖқи·ҜгҖӮ
 - еҺҹж–ҮжҸҗйҶ’дёҚиҰҒеӣӨиҙ§пјҢе№¶жҳҺзЎ®иҜҙеҸ·е•ҶеҸҜиғҪи·‘и·ҜпјҢиҝҷжң¬иә«иҜҙжҳҺжё йҒ“еҸҜйқ жҖ§е№¶дёҚй«ҳгҖӮ
 - иҜ„и®әеҢәжңүдәәиЎЁзӨәвҖңжІЎеӯҰеҲ°зІҫй«“вҖқжҲ–еҸӘжүҫеҲ°жӣҙиҙөзҡ„д»·ж јпјҢд№ҹеҸҚиҝҮжқҘиҜҙжҳҺиҝҷз§Қж–№жі•дёҚзЁіе®ҡгҖӮ
@@
-- иҝҷзҜҮеё–еӯҗйҖӮеҗҲдҝқз•ҷдёәдёҖдёӘж—¶й—ҙж•Ҹж„ҹгҖҒйЈҺйҷ©ж•Ҹж„ҹзҡ„жқҘжәҗж ·жң¬гҖӮ
-- дёҚйҖӮеҗҲд»ҺиҝҷзҜҮжқҘжәҗзӣҙжҺҘжҠҪжҰӮеҝөйЎөжҲ–дё»йўҳйЎөпјҢеӣ дёәе®ғзҡ„еҸҜеӨҚз”Ёд»·еҖјдҪҺпјҢиҖҢдё”еҫҲе®№жҳ“ж»‘еҗ‘ж“ҚдҪңжҖ§иҝҮејәзҡ„еҶ…е®№гҖӮ
-- еҰӮжһңеҗҺйқўз»§з»ӯз»ҙжҠӨпјҢжңҖеҗҲзҗҶзҡ„жҳҜеҸӘиЎҘдәӨеҸүжқҘжәҗжҲ–иЎҘйЈҺйҷ©иҜҙжҳҺпјҢиҖҢдёҚжҳҜжү©еҶҷжҲҗвҖңж•ҷзЁӢдҪ“зі»вҖқгҖӮ

# йЎәи—Өж‘ёз“ңејҸжҗңзҙў

## е®ҡд№ү

еңЁиҝҷеә§зҹҘиҜҶеә“йҮҢпјҢ`йЎәи—Өж‘ёз“ңејҸжҗңзҙў` жҢҮзҡ„жҳҜдёҚд»Һзӣ®ж Үзӯ”жЎҲжң¬иә«зӣҙжҺҘжҗңиө·пјҢиҖҢжҳҜе…ҲжүҫеҲ°дёҺзӣ®ж Үй«ҳеәҰзӣёе…ізҡ„еӨ–еӣҙзәҝзҙўпјҢеҶҚйЎәзқҖиҝҷдәӣзәҝзҙўдёҖжӯҘжӯҘзј©е°ҸиҢғеӣҙгҖҒйҖјиҝ‘зӣ®ж ҮгҖӮ

## дёәд»Җд№ҲйҮҚиҰҒ

еҫҲеӨҡдёңиҘҝд№ӢжүҖд»ҘйҡҫжүҫпјҢдёҚжҳҜеӣ дёәе®Ңе…ЁжІЎжңүе…¬ејҖз—•иҝ№пјҢиҖҢжҳҜеӣ дёәе®ғд»¬дёҚдјҡз”ЁжңҖзӣҙзҷҪзҡ„е…ій”®иҜҚжҡҙйңІиҮӘе·ұгҖӮиҝҷж—¶еҰӮжһңиҝҳеҸӘйқ вҖңзӣ®ж ҮиҜҚзӣҙжҗңвҖқпјҢжҗңзҙўж•ҲжһңеҫҖеҫҖеҫҲе·®гҖӮйЎәи—Өж‘ёз“ңејҸжҗңзҙўзҡ„д»·еҖје°ұеңЁдәҺпјҡе…ҲжүҫвҖңдјҡз•ҷдёӢз—•иҝ№зҡ„дәәгҖҒең°ж–№гҖҒе…іиҒ”зү©вҖқпјҢеҶҚеҸҚжҺЁзӣ®ж ҮгҖӮ

## е…ій”®иҰҒзӮ№

- е…ҲжүҫзәҝзҙўиҠӮзӮ№пјҢиҖҢдёҚжҳҜе…ҲжүҫжңҖз»Ҳзӯ”жЎҲгҖӮ
- зәҝзҙўиҠӮзӮ№еҸҜд»ҘжҳҜе№ҝе‘ҠдҪҚгҖҒе”®еҗҺзҫӨгҖҒдёӘдәәдё»йЎөгҖҒеҗҢзұ»з«ҷзӮ№гҖҒи®Ёи®әеҢәгҖҒе…ій”®иҜҚз»„еҗҲгҖҒеҹҹеҗҚзүҮж®өгҖӮ
- жҗңзҙўеј•ж“Һзҡ„дҪңз”ЁдёҚеҸӘжҳҜжүҫзҪ‘йЎөпјҢиҝҳиғҪеё®еҠ©дҪ йӘҢиҜҒвҖңе“ӘдәӣеҹҹеҗҚгҖҒе…ій”®иҜҚгҖҒеңәжҷҜеҪјжӯӨејәе…іиҒ”вҖқгҖӮ
- `site:` иҝҷзұ»жҗңзҙўиҜӯжі•зҡ„д»·еҖјпјҢеңЁдәҺжҠҠжҗңзҙўиҢғеӣҙд»Һж•ҙдёӘдә’иҒ”зҪ‘зј©еҲ°жҹҗдёӘе·ІзҹҘз«ҷзӮ№жҲ–жҹҗзұ»з«ҷзӮ№гҖӮ
- зңҹжӯЈжңүж•Ҳзҡ„йғЁеҲҶеҫҖеҫҖдёҚжҳҜжҹҗдёӘеӣәе®ҡй“ҫжҺҘпјҢиҖҢжҳҜвҖңеҰӮдҪ•жһ„йҖ дёӢдёҖиҪ®жҹҘиҜўвҖқгҖӮ

## иҫ№з•Ң

- е®ғдёҚзӯүдәҺвҖңжҗңеҫ—жӣҙж·ұвҖқжҲ–вҖңжҗңеҫ—жӣҙд№…вҖқгҖӮ
- е®ғд№ҹдёҚзӯүдәҺзҒ°иүІжё йҒ“жҗңзҙўжң¬иә«пјӣзҒ°иүІжё йҒ“еҸӘжҳҜиҝҷз§Қж–№жі•зҡ„дёҖдёӘдҪҝз”ЁеңәжҷҜгҖӮ
- иҝҷз§Қж–№жі•еҸҜд»Ҙз”ЁдәҺжүҫиө„ж–ҷгҖҒжүҫзӨҫеҢәи®Ёи®әгҖҒжүҫжЎҲдҫӢгҖҒжүҫеҺҶеҸІйЎөйқўпјҢдҪҶе…·дҪ“дҪҝз”Ёд»ҚиҰҒиҖғиҷ‘еҗҲи§„гҖҒеҸҜйқ жҖ§е’ҢйЈҺйҷ©гҖӮ

## еёёи§ҒеҲҶжӯ§

- дёҖз§ҚеҒҡжі•еҒҸеҗ‘зӣҙжҺҘе…ій”®иҜҚжЈҖзҙўпјҢдјҳзӮ№жҳҜеҝ«пјҢзјәзӮ№жҳҜе®№жҳ“еҚЎеңЁиЎЁеұӮз»“жһңгҖӮ
- еҸҰдёҖз§ҚеҒҡжі•еҒҸеҗ‘е…Ҳе»әзәҝзҙўзҪ‘з»ңпјҢеҶҚйҖҗжӯҘзј©е°ҸиҢғеӣҙпјҢдјҳзӮ№жҳҜжӣҙе®№жҳ“ж‘ёеҲ°йҡҗи—Ҹз»“жһ„пјҢзјәзӮ№жҳҜжӣҙдҫқиө–еҲӨж–ӯеҠӣгҖӮ
- еңЁдҝЎжҒҜзЁҖз–ҸгҖҒе…ій”®иҜҚж··д№ұгҖҒз»“жһңй«ҳеәҰдёӘжҖ§еҢ–зҡ„еңәжҷҜдёӢпјҢеҗҺиҖ…йҖҡеёёжӣҙжңүж•ҲгҖӮ

import os,re
root='/Users/liuyifu/Documents/123/MyLLMWiki'
pat=re.compile(r'\[[^\]]*\]\((?!https?://|mailto:|#)([^)]+)\)')
missing=[]
for base,_,files in os.walk(root):
    for fn in files:
        if not fn.endswith('.md'): continue
        p=os.path.join(base,fn)
        rel=os.path.relpath(p,root)
        txt=open(p,'r',encoding='utf-8').read()
        for m in pat.finditer(txt):
            target=m.group(1).strip().split('#',1)[0]
            if not target:
                continue
            full=os.path.normpath(os.path.join(base,target))
            if not os.path.exists(full):
                missing.append((rel,target))
for rel,target in missing:
    print(f'{rel}\t{target}\tMISSING')
print('TOTAL_MISSING', len(missing))
PY

# йЎәи—Өж‘ёз“ңејҸжҗңзҙў

## е®ҡд№ү

еңЁиҝҷеә§зҹҘиҜҶеә“йҮҢпјҢ`йЎәи—Өж‘ёз“ңејҸжҗңзҙў` жҢҮзҡ„жҳҜдёҚд»Һзӣ®ж Үзӯ”жЎҲжң¬иә«зӣҙжҺҘжҗңиө·пјҢиҖҢжҳҜе…ҲжүҫеҲ°дёҺзӣ®ж Үй«ҳеәҰзӣёе…ізҡ„еӨ–еӣҙзәҝзҙўпјҢеҶҚйЎәзқҖиҝҷдәӣзәҝзҙўдёҖжӯҘжӯҘзј©е°ҸиҢғеӣҙгҖҒйҖјиҝ‘зӣ®ж ҮгҖӮ

## дёәд»Җд№ҲйҮҚиҰҒ

еҫҲеӨҡдёңиҘҝд№ӢжүҖд»ҘйҡҫжүҫпјҢдёҚжҳҜеӣ дёәе®Ңе…ЁжІЎжңүе…¬ејҖз—•иҝ№пјҢиҖҢжҳҜеӣ дёәе®ғд»¬дёҚдјҡз”ЁжңҖзӣҙзҷҪзҡ„е…ій”®иҜҚжҡҙйңІиҮӘе·ұгҖӮиҝҷж—¶еҰӮжһңиҝҳеҸӘйқ вҖңзӣ®ж ҮиҜҚзӣҙжҗңвҖқпјҢжҗңзҙўж•ҲжһңеҫҖеҫҖеҫҲе·®гҖӮйЎәи—Өж‘ёз“ңејҸжҗңзҙўзҡ„д»·еҖје°ұеңЁдәҺпјҡе…ҲжүҫвҖңдјҡз•ҷдёӢз—•иҝ№зҡ„дәәгҖҒең°ж–№гҖҒе…іиҒ”зү©вҖқпјҢеҶҚеҸҚжҺЁзӣ®ж ҮгҖӮ

## е…ій”®иҰҒзӮ№

- е…ҲжүҫзәҝзҙўиҠӮзӮ№пјҢиҖҢдёҚжҳҜе…ҲжүҫжңҖз»Ҳзӯ”жЎҲгҖӮ
- зәҝзҙўиҠӮзӮ№еҸҜд»ҘжҳҜе№ҝе‘ҠдҪҚгҖҒе”®еҗҺзҫӨгҖҒдёӘдәәдё»йЎөгҖҒеҗҢзұ»з«ҷзӮ№гҖҒи®Ёи®әеҢәгҖҒе…ій”®иҜҚз»„еҗҲгҖҒеҹҹеҗҚзүҮж®өгҖӮ
- жҗңзҙўеј•ж“Һзҡ„дҪңз”ЁдёҚеҸӘжҳҜжүҫзҪ‘йЎөпјҢиҝҳиғҪеё®еҠ©дҪ йӘҢиҜҒвҖңе“ӘдәӣеҹҹеҗҚгҖҒе…ій”®иҜҚгҖҒеңәжҷҜеҪјжӯӨејәе…іиҒ”вҖқгҖӮ
- `site:` иҝҷзұ»жҗңзҙўиҜӯжі•зҡ„д»·еҖјпјҢеңЁдәҺжҠҠжҗңзҙўиҢғеӣҙд»Һж•ҙдёӘдә’иҒ”зҪ‘зј©еҲ°жҹҗдёӘе·ІзҹҘз«ҷзӮ№жҲ–жҹҗзұ»з«ҷзӮ№гҖӮ
- зңҹжӯЈжңүж•Ҳзҡ„йғЁеҲҶеҫҖеҫҖдёҚжҳҜжҹҗдёӘеӣәе®ҡй“ҫжҺҘпјҢиҖҢжҳҜвҖңеҰӮдҪ•жһ„йҖ дёӢдёҖиҪ®жҹҘиҜўвҖқгҖӮ

## иҫ№з•Ң

- е®ғдёҚзӯүдәҺвҖңжҗңеҫ—жӣҙж·ұвҖқжҲ–вҖңжҗңеҫ—жӣҙд№…вҖқгҖӮ
- е®ғд№ҹдёҚзӯүдәҺзҒ°иүІжё йҒ“жҗңзҙўжң¬иә«пјӣзҒ°иүІжё йҒ“еҸӘжҳҜиҝҷз§Қж–№жі•зҡ„дёҖдёӘдҪҝз”ЁеңәжҷҜгҖӮ
- иҝҷз§Қж–№жі•еҸҜд»Ҙз”ЁдәҺжүҫиө„ж–ҷгҖҒжүҫзӨҫеҢәи®Ёи®әгҖҒжүҫжЎҲдҫӢгҖҒжүҫеҺҶеҸІйЎөйқўпјҢдҪҶе…·дҪ“дҪҝз”Ёд»ҚиҰҒиҖғиҷ‘еҗҲи§„гҖҒеҸҜйқ жҖ§е’ҢйЈҺйҷ©гҖӮ

## еёёи§ҒеҲҶжӯ§

- дёҖз§ҚеҒҡжі•еҒҸеҗ‘зӣҙжҺҘе…ій”®иҜҚжЈҖзҙўпјҢдјҳзӮ№жҳҜеҝ«пјҢзјәзӮ№жҳҜе®№жҳ“еҚЎеңЁиЎЁеұӮз»“жһңгҖӮ
- еҸҰдёҖз§ҚеҒҡжі•еҒҸеҗ‘е…Ҳе»әзәҝзҙўзҪ‘з»ңпјҢеҶҚйҖҗжӯҘзј©е°ҸиҢғеӣҙпјҢдјҳзӮ№жҳҜжӣҙе®№жҳ“ж‘ёеҲ°йҡҗи—Ҹз»“жһ„пјҢзјәзӮ№жҳҜжӣҙдҫқиө–еҲӨж–ӯеҠӣгҖӮ
- еңЁдҝЎжҒҜзЁҖз–ҸгҖҒе…ій”®иҜҚж··д№ұгҖҒз»“жһңй«ҳеәҰдёӘжҖ§еҢ–зҡ„еңәжҷҜдёӢпјҢеҗҺиҖ…йҖҡеёёжӣҙжңүж•ҲгҖӮ

# 6.5 еқ— Plus дёҺ 2.5 еқ— Team зҡ„иҺ·еҸ–жё йҒ“

## дёҖеҸҘиҜқжҖ»з»“

иҝҷжҳҜдёҖзҜҮеҖҹдҪҺд»· Plus/Team жё йҒ“дёәдҫӢи®ІвҖңжҖҺд№ҲйЎәи—Өж‘ёз“ңжҗңдҝЎжҒҜвҖқзҡ„и®әеқӣеё–еӯҗпјҢжё йҒ“жң¬иә«й«ҳеәҰдёҚзЁіе®ҡпјҢдҪҶе…¶дёӯзҡ„жҗңзҙўи·Ҝеҫ„е’Ңзәҝзҙўжһ„йҖ ж–№ејҸжңүдёҖе®ҡеҸҜеӨҚз”Ёд»·еҖјгҖӮ

## ж‘ҳиҰҒ

- жқҘжәҗжҳҜ linux.do дёҠзҡ„дёҖзҜҮзҰҸеҲ©зҫҠжҜӣеё–пјҢдҪңиҖ…дёә `revival`пјҢж—¶й—ҙжҳҜ 2026-04-15гҖӮ
- её–еӯҗдёҚжҳҜзӣҙжҺҘиҙҙеӣәе®ҡеә—й“әй“ҫжҺҘпјҢиҖҢжҳҜеңЁи®ІдёҖз§ҚвҖңйЎәи—Өж‘ёз“ңвҖқзҡ„жҗңзҙўж–№жі•гҖӮ
- дҪңиҖ…жҠҠзӣ®ж Үжё йҒ“еҲҶжҲҗдёӨзұ»пјҡTG еҸ·е•Ҷе’ҢеҸ‘еҚЎе№іеҸ°пјҢе№¶е»әи®®йҖҡиҝҮе№ҝе‘ҠдҪҚгҖҒе”®еҗҺзҫӨгҖҒжҗңзҙўеј•ж“Һзӯүж–№ејҸй—ҙжҺҘеҸ‘зҺ°гҖӮ
- её–еӯҗиҝҳжҸҗеҲ°дёҖдәӣдҪҺд»·еҢәй—ҙе’ҢиҙЁдҝқиҜҙжі•пјҢдҪҶиҝҷдәӣдҝЎжҒҜжҳҺжҳҫе…·жңүжһҒејәж—¶ж•ҲжҖ§пјҢиҖҢдё”йҡҸж—¶еҸҜиғҪеӨұж•ҲгҖӮ
- иҜ„и®әеҢәеҸҚйҰҲе№¶дёҚдёҖиҮҙпјҢжңүдәәи§үеҫ—ж–№жі•жңүеҗҜеҸ‘пјҢд№ҹжңүдәәиЎЁзӨәиҮӘе·ұз…§зқҖиҜ•дәҶд»Қз„¶еҸӘжүҫеҲ°жӣҙиҙөзҡ„з»“жһңгҖӮ
- иҝҷзұ»еҶ…е®№дҪңдёәжё йҒ“ж ·жң¬жң¬иә«дёҚйҖӮеҗҲеҪ“жҺЁиҚҗж–№жЎҲпјҢдҪҶе…¶дёӯвҖңзәҝзҙўй©ұеҠЁжҗңзҙўвҖқзҡ„йғЁеҲҶеҖјеҫ—еҚ•зӢ¬жҸҗзӮјгҖӮ

## е…ій”®зӮ№

- иҝҷд»ҪжқҘжәҗжңҖеҖјеҫ—дҝқз•ҷзҡ„пјҢдёҚжҳҜдҪҺд»·жё йҒ“з»“и®әпјҢиҖҢжҳҜвҖңжҗңзҙўе’ҢеҸ‘зҺ°и·Ҝеҫ„вҖқгҖӮ
- е®ғејәи°ғзҡ„жҳҜеҰӮдҪ•йҖҡиҝҮзәҝзҙўиҠӮзӮ№йҖҗжӯҘзј©е°ҸиҢғеӣҙпјҢиҖҢдёҚжҳҜдҫқиө–жҹҗдёӘеӣәе®ҡе…ҘеҸЈгҖӮ
- её–еӯҗйҮҢзҡ„д»·ж јгҖҒиҙЁдҝқгҖҒеҸ·е•Ҷеӯҳжҙ»зҠ¶жҖҒйғҪй«ҳеәҰжіўеҠЁпјҢдёҚиғҪжҢүй•ҝжңҹдәӢе®һи®°гҖӮ

- еҺҹж–ҮжҳҺзЎ®жҠҠзӣ®ж Үж”ҫеңЁ `Plus`гҖҒ`Team` зӯүдҪҺд»·иҺ·еҸ–жё йҒ“дёҠпјҢе№¶жҠҠ `TG еҸ·е•Ҷ` е’Ң `еҚЎзҪ‘` еҪ“жҲҗдё»иҰҒзәҝзҙўжқҘжәҗгҖӮ
- еҺҹж–ҮжҸҗеҲ°йҖҡиҝҮе№ҝе‘ҠдҪҚгҖҒе”®еҗҺзҫӨе’ҢжҗңзҙўиҜӯжі•зј©е°ҸиҢғеӣҙпјҢдҪҶжІЎжңүжҸҗдҫӣзЁіе®ҡе®ҳж–№е…ҘеҸЈгҖӮ
- еҺҹж–Үз»ҷеҮәзҡ„зңҹжӯЈеҸҜиҝҒз§»йғЁеҲҶпјҢжҳҜвҖңе…Ҳжүҫдјҡжү“е№ҝе‘Ҡзҡ„дәәе’Ңең°ж–№пјҢеҶҚд»Һе…іиҒ”з«ҷзӮ№е’ҢеҹҹеҗҚйҮҢз»§з»ӯжҗңвҖқзҡ„зәҝзҙўжһ„йҖ жҖқи·ҜгҖӮ
- еҺҹж–ҮжҸҗйҶ’дёҚиҰҒеӣӨиҙ§пјҢе№¶жҳҺзЎ®иҜҙеҸ·е•ҶеҸҜиғҪи·‘и·ҜпјҢиҝҷжң¬иә«иҜҙжҳҺжё йҒ“вҖҰ739 chars truncatedвҖҰ[иҮӘиҝӣеҢ– Agent](../wiki/concepts/иҮӘиҝӣеҢ– Agent.md)пҪңеҗҢд№үеҗҚпјҡ`Self-Evolving Agent`пҪңзӣёе…іжқҘжәҗж•°пјҡ2пҪңзӣёе…ідё»йўҳж•°пјҡ0пҪңжҳҜеҗҰеӯӨз«ӢпјҡеҗҰ

# йЎәи—Өж‘ёз“ңејҸжҗңзҙў

## е®ҡд№ү

еңЁиҝҷеә§зҹҘиҜҶеә“йҮҢпјҢ`йЎәи—Өж‘ёз“ңејҸжҗңзҙў` жҢҮзҡ„жҳҜдёҚд»Һзӣ®ж Үзӯ”жЎҲжң¬иә«зӣҙжҺҘжҗңиө·пјҢиҖҢжҳҜе…ҲжүҫеҲ°дёҺзӣ®ж Үй«ҳеәҰзӣёе…ізҡ„еӨ–еӣҙзәҝзҙўпјҢеҶҚйЎәзқҖиҝҷдәӣзәҝзҙўдёҖжӯҘжӯҘзј©е°ҸиҢғеӣҙгҖҒйҖјиҝ‘зӣ®ж ҮгҖӮ

## дёәд»Җд№ҲйҮҚиҰҒ

еҫҲеӨҡдёңиҘҝд№ӢжүҖд»ҘйҡҫжүҫпјҢдёҚжҳҜеӣ дёәе®Ңе…ЁжІЎжңүе…¬ејҖз—•иҝ№пјҢиҖҢжҳҜеӣ дёәе®ғд»¬дёҚдјҡз”ЁжңҖзӣҙзҷҪзҡ„е…ій”®иҜҚжҡҙйңІиҮӘе·ұгҖӮиҝҷж—¶еҰӮжһңиҝҳеҸӘйқ вҖңзӣ®ж ҮиҜҚзӣҙжҗңвҖқпјҢжҗңзҙўж•ҲжһңеҫҖеҫҖеҫҲе·®гҖӮйЎәи—Өж‘ёз“ңејҸжҗңзҙўзҡ„д»·еҖје°ұеңЁдәҺпјҡе…ҲжүҫвҖңдјҡз•ҷдёӢз—•иҝ№зҡ„дәәгҖҒең°ж–№гҖҒе…іиҒ”зү©вҖқпјҢеҶҚеҸҚжҺЁзӣ®ж ҮгҖӮ

## е…ій”®иҰҒзӮ№

- е…ҲжүҫзәҝзҙўиҠӮзӮ№пјҢиҖҢдёҚжҳҜе…ҲжүҫжңҖз»Ҳзӯ”жЎҲгҖӮ
- зәҝзҙўиҠӮзӮ№еҸҜд»ҘжҳҜе№ҝе‘ҠдҪҚгҖҒе”®еҗҺзҫӨгҖҒдёӘдәәдё»йЎөгҖҒеҗҢзұ»з«ҷзӮ№гҖҒи®Ёи®әеҢәгҖҒе…ій”®иҜҚз»„еҗҲгҖҒеҹҹеҗҚзүҮж®өгҖӮ
- жҗңзҙўеј•ж“Һзҡ„дҪңз”ЁдёҚеҸӘжҳҜжүҫзҪ‘йЎөпјҢиҝҳиғҪеё®еҠ©дҪ йӘҢиҜҒвҖңе“ӘдәӣеҹҹеҗҚгҖҒе…ій”®иҜҚгҖҒеңәжҷҜеҪјжӯӨејәе…іиҒ”вҖқгҖӮ
- `site:` иҝҷзұ»жҗңзҙўиҜӯжі•зҡ„д»·еҖјпјҢеңЁдәҺжҠҠжҗңзҙўиҢғеӣҙд»Һж•ҙдёӘдә’иҒ”зҪ‘зј©еҲ°жҹҗдёӘе·ІзҹҘз«ҷзӮ№жҲ–жҹҗзұ»з«ҷзӮ№гҖӮ
- зңҹжӯЈжңүж•Ҳзҡ„йғЁеҲҶеҫҖеҫҖдёҚжҳҜжҹҗдёӘеӣәе®ҡй“ҫжҺҘпјҢиҖҢжҳҜвҖңеҰӮдҪ•жһ„йҖ дёӢдёҖиҪ®жҹҘиҜўвҖқгҖӮ

## иҫ№з•Ң

- е®ғдёҚзӯүдәҺвҖңжҗңеҫ—жӣҙж·ұвҖқжҲ–вҖңжҗңеҫ—жӣҙд№…вҖқгҖӮ
- е®ғд№ҹдёҚзӯүдәҺзҒ°иүІжё йҒ“жҗңзҙўжң¬иә«пјӣзҒ°иүІжё йҒ“еҸӘжҳҜиҝҷз§Қж–№жі•зҡ„дёҖдёӘдҪҝз”ЁеңәжҷҜгҖӮ
- иҝҷз§Қж–№жі•еҸҜд»Ҙз”ЁдәҺжүҫиө„ж–ҷгҖҒжүҫзӨҫеҢәи®Ёи®әгҖҒжүҫжЎҲдҫӢгҖҒжүҫеҺҶеҸІйЎөйқўпјҢдҪҶе…·дҪ“дҪҝз”Ёд»ҚиҰҒиҖғиҷ‘еҗҲи§„гҖҒеҸҜйқ жҖ§е’ҢйЈҺйҷ©гҖӮ

## еёёи§ҒеҲҶжӯ§

- дёҖз§ҚеҒҡжі•еҒҸеҗ‘зӣҙжҺҘе…ій”®иҜҚжЈҖзҙўпјҢдјҳзӮ№жҳҜеҝ«пјҢзјәзӮ№жҳҜе®№жҳ“еҚЎеңЁиЎЁеұӮз»“жһңгҖӮ
- еҸҰдёҖз§ҚеҒҡжі•еҒҸеҗ‘е…Ҳе»әзәҝзҙўзҪ‘з»ңпјҢеҶҚйҖҗжӯҘзј©е°ҸиҢғеӣҙпјҢдјҳзӮ№жҳҜжӣҙе®№жҳ“ж‘ёеҲ°йҡҗи—Ҹз»“жһ„пјҢзјәзӮ№жҳҜжӣҙдҫқиө–еҲӨж–ӯеҠӣгҖӮ
- еңЁдҝЎжҒҜзЁҖз–ҸгҖҒе…ій”®иҜҚж··д№ұгҖҒз»“жһңй«ҳеәҰдёӘжҖ§еҢ–зҡ„еңәжҷҜдёӢпјҢеҗҺиҖ…йҖҡеёёжӣҙжңүж•ҲгҖӮ

# 6.5 еқ— Plus дёҺ 2.5 еқ— Team зҡ„иҺ·еҸ–жё йҒ“

## дёҖеҸҘиҜқжҖ»з»“

иҝҷжҳҜдёҖзҜҮеҖҹдҪҺд»· Plus/Team жё йҒ“дёәдҫӢи®ІвҖңжҖҺд№ҲйЎәи—Өж‘ёз“ңжҗңдҝЎжҒҜвҖқзҡ„и®әеқӣеё–еӯҗпјҢжё йҒ“жң¬иә«й«ҳеәҰдёҚзЁіе®ҡпјҢдҪҶе…¶дёӯзҡ„жҗңзҙўи·Ҝеҫ„е’Ңзәҝзҙўжһ„йҖ ж–№ејҸжңүдёҖе®ҡеҸҜеӨҚз”Ёд»·еҖјгҖӮ

## ж‘ҳиҰҒ

- жқҘжәҗжҳҜ linux.do дёҠзҡ„дёҖзҜҮзҰҸеҲ©зҫҠжҜӣеё–пјҢдҪңиҖ…дёә `revival`пјҢж—¶й—ҙжҳҜ 2026-04-15гҖӮ
- её–еӯҗдёҚжҳҜзӣҙжҺҘиҙҙеӣәе®ҡеә—й“әй“ҫжҺҘпјҢиҖҢжҳҜеңЁи®ІдёҖз§ҚвҖңйЎәи—Өж‘ёз“ңвҖқзҡ„жҗңзҙўж–№жі•гҖӮ
- дҪңиҖ…жҠҠзӣ®ж Үжё йҒ“еҲҶжҲҗдёӨзұ»пјҡTG еҸ·е•Ҷе’ҢеҸ‘еҚЎе№іеҸ°пјҢе№¶е»әи®®йҖҡиҝҮе№ҝе‘ҠдҪҚгҖҒе”®еҗҺзҫӨгҖҒжҗңзҙўеј•ж“Һзӯүж–№ејҸй—ҙжҺҘеҸ‘зҺ°гҖӮ
- её–еӯҗиҝҳжҸҗеҲ°дёҖдәӣдҪҺд»·еҢәй—ҙе’ҢиҙЁдҝқиҜҙжі•пјҢдҪҶиҝҷдәӣдҝЎжҒҜжҳҺжҳҫе…·жңүжһҒејәж—¶ж•ҲжҖ§пјҢиҖҢдё”йҡҸж—¶еҸҜиғҪеӨұж•ҲгҖӮ
- иҜ„и®әеҢәеҸҚйҰҲе№¶дёҚдёҖиҮҙпјҢжңүдәәи§үеҫ—ж–№жі•жңүеҗҜеҸ‘пјҢд№ҹжңүдәәиЎЁзӨәиҮӘе·ұз…§зқҖиҜ•дәҶд»Қз„¶еҸӘжүҫеҲ°жӣҙиҙөзҡ„з»“жһңгҖӮ
- иҝҷзұ»еҶ…е®№дҪңдёәжё йҒ“ж ·жң¬жң¬иә«дёҚйҖӮеҗҲеҪ“жҺЁиҚҗж–№жЎҲпјҢдҪҶе…¶дёӯвҖңзәҝзҙўй©ұеҠЁжҗңзҙўвҖқзҡ„йғЁеҲҶеҖјеҫ—еҚ•зӢ¬жҸҗзӮјгҖӮ

## е…ій”®зӮ№

- иҝҷд»ҪжқҘжәҗжңҖеҖјеҫ—дҝқз•ҷзҡ„пјҢдёҚжҳҜдҪҺд»·жё йҒ“з»“и®әпјҢиҖҢжҳҜвҖңжҗңзҙўе’ҢеҸ‘зҺ°и·Ҝеҫ„вҖқгҖӮ
- е®ғејәи°ғзҡ„жҳҜеҰӮдҪ•йҖҡиҝҮзәҝзҙўиҠӮзӮ№йҖҗжӯҘзј©е°ҸиҢғеӣҙпјҢиҖҢдёҚжҳҜдҫқиө–жҹҗдёӘеӣәе®ҡе…ҘеҸЈгҖӮ
- её–еӯҗйҮҢзҡ„д»·ж јгҖҒиҙЁдҝқгҖҒеҸ·е•Ҷеӯҳжҙ»зҠ¶жҖҒйғҪй«ҳеәҰжіўеҠЁпјҢдёҚиғҪжҢүй•ҝжңҹдәӢе®һи®°гҖӮ
- иҝҷзұ»жқҘжәҗиҝҳеёҰжңүжҳҺжҳҫзҡ„еҗҲи§„гҖҒйЈҺжҺ§е’ҢиҜҲйӘ—йЈҺйҷ©пјҢеӣ жӯӨжӣҙйҖӮеҗҲдҪңдёәвҖңж–№жі•ж ·жң¬ + йЈҺйҷ©ж ·жң¬вҖқпјҢиҖҢдёҚжҳҜиҙӯд№°жҢҮеҚ—гҖӮ

## иҜҒжҚ®жҲ–еҺҹж–Үзәҝзҙў

- еҺҹж–ҮжҳҺзЎ®жҠҠзӣ®ж Үж”ҫеңЁ `Plus`гҖҒ`Team` зӯүдҪҺд»·иҺ·еҸ–жё йҒ“дёҠпјҢе№¶жҠҠ `TG еҸ·е•Ҷ` е’Ң `еҚЎзҪ‘` еҪ“жҲҗдё»иҰҒзәҝзҙўжқҘжәҗгҖӮ
- еҺҹж–ҮжҸҗеҲ°йҖҡиҝҮе№ҝе‘ҠдҪҚгҖҒе”®еҗҺзҫӨе’ҢжҗңзҙўиҜӯжі•зј©е°ҸиҢғеӣҙпјҢдҪҶжІЎжңүжҸҗдҫӣзЁіе®ҡе®ҳж–№е…ҘеҸЈгҖӮ
- еҺҹж–Үз»ҷеҮәзҡ„зңҹжӯЈеҸҜиҝҒз§»йғЁеҲҶпјҢжҳҜвҖңе…Ҳжүҫдјҡжү“е№ҝе‘Ҡзҡ„дәәе’Ңең°ж–№пјҢеҶҚд»Һе…іиҒ”з«ҷзӮ№е’ҢеҹҹеҗҚйҮҢз»§з»ӯжҗңвҖқзҡ„зәҝзҙўжһ„йҖ жҖқи·ҜгҖӮ
- еҺҹж–ҮжҸҗйҶ’дёҚиҰҒеӣӨиҙ§пјҢе№¶жҳҺзЎ®иҜҙеҸ·е•ҶеҸҜиғҪи·‘и·ҜпјҢиҝҷжң¬иә«иҜҙжҳҺжё йҒ“еҸҜйқ жҖ§е№¶дёҚй«ҳгҖӮ
- иҜ„и®әеҢәжңүдәәиЎЁзӨәвҖңжІЎеӯҰеҲ°зІҫй«“вҖқжҲ–еҸӘжүҫеҲ°жӣҙиҙөзҡ„д»·ж јпјҢд№ҹеҸҚиҝҮжқҘиҜҙжҳҺиҝҷз§Қж–№жі•дёҚзЁіе®ҡгҖӮ

## жҲ‘зҡ„еҲӨж–ӯ

- иҝҷзҜҮеё–еӯҗйҖӮеҗҲеҗҢж—¶дҝқз•ҷдёӨеұӮж„Ҹд№үпјҡдёҖеұӮжҳҜй«ҳж—¶ж•ҲгҖҒй«ҳйЈҺйҷ©зҡ„жё йҒ“ж ·жң¬пјӣеҸҰдёҖеұӮжҳҜжӣҙеҸҜеӨҚз”Ёзҡ„жҗңзҙўж–№жі•ж ·жң¬гҖӮ
- дёҚйҖӮеҗҲжҠҠжё йҒ“йғЁеҲҶжү©жҲҗж“ҚдҪңжҖ§ж•ҷзЁӢпјҢдҪҶйҖӮеҗҲжҠҠвҖңйЎәи—Өж‘ёз“ңејҸжҗңзҙўвҖқжҠҪжҲҗжӣҙйҖҡз”Ёзҡ„жҰӮеҝөйЎөгҖӮ
- еҰӮжһңеҗҺйқўз»§з»ӯз»ҙжҠӨпјҢжңҖеҖјеҫ—иЎҘзҡ„жҳҜжӣҙеӨҡеңәжҷҜдёӢзҡ„еҗҢзұ»жҗңзҙўжЎҲдҫӢпјҢиҖҢдёҚжҳҜиЎҘжӣҙеӨҡеҸ·е•ҶжҲ–еҚЎзҪ‘з»ҶиҠӮгҖӮ

settings.developer_instructions:
# Collaboration Mode: Default

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

The `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.

payload.user_instructions:
# е…ЁеұҖеҚҸдҪңзәҰе®ҡпјҲзі»з»ҹзә§пјү

1. жҲ‘жҸҗеҮәзҡ„жүҖжңүй—®йўҳпјҢйғҪдёҚиҰҒзӣҙжҺҘж”№д»Јз Ғпјӣеҝ…йЎ»е…Ҳз»ҷеҮәж–№жЎҲ/ж”№еҠЁзӮ№пјҢе№¶дёҺжҲ‘зЎ®и®ӨеҗҺеҶҚејҖе§Ӣдҝ®ж”№гҖӮ
2. еҜ№жҲ‘жҸҗдҫӣзҡ„и®ҫи®ЎеӣҫпјҡеҝҪз•Ҙе…¶дёӯзҡ„иҷҡзәҝиҫ№жЎҶпјҲдёҚдҪңдёәйңҖиҰҒе®һзҺ°зҡ„ UI/еёғеұҖиҫ№з•ҢпјүгҖӮ
3. й»ҳи®ӨдҪҝз”Ё `shuorenhua` зҡ„иЎЁиҫҫи§„еҲҷеӣһзӯ”пјҡдјҳе…ҲиҜҙдәәиҜқгҖҒеҺ»жЁЎжқҝж„ҹгҖҒиҮӘз„¶дёҖзӮ№пјҢеҮҸе°‘ AI еҘ—иҜқе’Ңз©әжҙһиҝһжҺҘиҜҚпјӣдҪҶдёҚиғҪж”№еҸҳдәӢе®һгҖҒз»“и®әгҖҒж•°жҚ®гҖҒжңҜиҜӯе’Ңд»Јз ҒиҜӯд№үгҖӮйҷӨйқһжҲ‘жҳҺзЎ®иҰҒжұӮдҝқз•ҷжӯЈејҸ/жЁЎжқҝеҢ–иЎЁиҫҫпјҢеҗҰеҲҷйғҪжҢүиҝҷдёӘи§„еҲҷжү§иЎҢгҖӮ
const additionalDeveloperPrompt = `# еҝ…йЎ»йҒөе®Ҳзҡ„иЎҢдёәи§„иҢғ\n
* еҝ…йЎ»дҪҝз”Ёдёӯж–Үеӣһзӯ”\n
* еңЁйҒҮеҲ°йңҖиҰҒжҺўзҙўжәҗз Ғзҡ„д»»еҠЎж—¶пјҢжӢҶеҲҶеҮәе…ій”®иҜҚпјҢиҝӣиЎҢgrepжҗңзҙўпјҢдёҖж¬ЎжҖ§иҜ»еҸ–жүҖжңүзӣёе…іж–Үд»¶иҝӣиЎҢеҲҶжһҗпјҢиҜ»ж–Үд»¶ж—¶еҰӮжһңж–Үд»¶д»Јз ҒжІЎжңүи¶…иҝҮ600иЎҢпјҢдёҚиҰҒеҲҶж®өиҜ»еҸ–пјҢдёҖж¬Ўе…ЁйғЁиҜ»е…ҘжүҖжңүеҶ…е®№пјҢеҝ…йЎ»еңЁжңҖе°‘зҡ„дәӨдә’дјҡиҜқж¬Ўж•°еҶ…е®ҢжҲҗе·ҘдҪң\n
* зј–иҫ‘ж–Үд»¶ж—¶пјҢеҰӮжһңж¶үеҸҠеҲ°ж•ҙдёӘж–Үд»¶зҡ„иҝҮеӨҡж¬Ўдҝ®ж”№гҖӮдёҚиҰҒдҪҝз”ЁEditе·Ҙе…·еӨҡж¬Ўдҝ®ж”№пјҢиҖҢеҝ…йЎ»дҪҝз”ЁWriteе·Ҙе…·дёҖж¬ЎжҖ§з”ҹжҲҗж–Үд»¶гҖӮз”ҹжҲҗд»Јз Ғж—¶пјҢжңҖеӨ§йҷҗеәҰдҝқжҢҒзҺ°жңүд»Јз ҒпјҢдёҚиҰҒйҡҸж„Ҹж јејҸеҢ–д»Јз ҒпјҢеҸӘдҝ®ж”№жҲ–еўһеҠ еҝ…иҰҒзҡ„йғЁеҲҶ\n
* жЁЎд»ҝclaudeзҡ„йЈҺж јеӣһеӨҚз”ЁжҲ·\n
* з”ҹжҲҗд»Јз Ғж—¶пјҢеҸҜиҜ»жҖ§дёҖе®ҡиҰҒеҘҪпјҢдҫҝдәҺдәәзұ»зҗҶи§ЈпјҢдҪҶдёҚйңҖиҰҒеҫҲеӨҡжіЁйҮҠгҖӮеңЁдҝқиҜҒйҖ»иҫ‘жё…жҷ°пјҢжӯЈзЎ®зҡ„еҹәзЎҖдёҠиҰҒеҒҡеҲ°д»Јз ҒзІҫзӮјпјҢз”ЁжңҖе°‘зҡ„д»Јз ҒеҒҡжңҖеӨҡзҡ„дәӢжғ…пјҢдҪҶжҳҜдёҚиғҪз ҙеқҸеҸҜиҜ»жҖ§гҖӮ\n`
* жҜҸж¬ЎеӣһеӨҚйғҪе–ҠжҲ‘lelele

# Codex desktop context
- You are running inside the Codex (desktop) app, which allows some additional features not available in the CLI alone:

### Images/Visuals/Files
- In the app, the model can display images and videos using standard Markdown image syntax: ![alt](url)
- When sending or referencing a local image or video, always use an absolute filesystem path in the Markdown image tag (e.g., ![alt](/absolute/path.png)); relative paths and plain text will not render the media.
- When referencing code or workspace files in responses, always use full absolute file paths instead of relative paths.
- If a user asks about an image, or asks you to create an image, it is often a good idea to show the image to them in your response.
- Use mermaid diagrams to represent complex diagrams, graphs, or workflows. Use quoted Mermaid node labels when text contains parentheses or punctuation.
- Return web URLs as Markdown links (e.g., [label](https://example.com)).

### Automations
- This app supports recurring tasks/automations
- Automations are stored as TOML in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml (not in SQLite). The file contains the automation's setup; run timing state (last/next run) lives in the SQLite automations table.

#### When to use the automation tool
- Only use the `automation_update` tool when the user explicitly asks for automation, a recurring run, or a repeated task.
- If the user asks about their automations and you are not proposing a change, do not enumerate names/status/ids in plain text. Fetch/list automations first and call `automation_update` with mode="view" for those ids; never invent ids.
- Never return raw RRULE strings in user-facing responses. If the user asks about their automations, respond using `automation_update` (e.g., with an "Open" button if you're not making changes).

#### Tool arguments
- Modes: view, suggested_update, suggested_create. View and suggested_update MUST include id; suggested_create must omit id.
- For view calls, id is required and other fields are optional (the UI can load details).
- For suggested_update/suggested_create, include name, prompt, rrule, kind, and status.
- For cron automations, include cwds, executionEnvironment, model, and reasoningEffort. cwds can be a JSON array or comma-separated string.
- Always come up with a short name for the automation. If the user does not give one, propose a short name and confirm.
- Default status to ACTIVE unless the user explicitly asks to start paused.
- Always interpret and schedule times in the user's locale time zone.

#### Prompting guidance
- Ask in plain language what it should do, when it should run, and which workspaces or thread it should use (if any), then map those answers into the tool call arguments.
- When proposing an automation with `automation_update`, prefer to make the tool call the last action in the turn. If brief user-facing prose is helpful, put it before the tool call, not after it. Avoid adding a separate closing message after the tool call unless the user specifically asked for extra explanation.
- The automation prompt should describe only the task itself. Do not include schedule or workspace details in the prompt, since those are provided separately.
- Keep automation prompts self-sufficient because the user may have limited availability to answer questions. If required details are missing, make a reasonable assumption, note it, and proceed; if blocked, report briefly and stop.
- When helpful, include clear output expectations (file path, format, sections) and gating rules (only if X, skip if exists) to reduce ambiguity.
- Automations should always open an inbox item.
- Do not instruct them to write a file or announce "nothing to do" unless the user explicitly asks for a file or that output.
- When mentioning skills in automation prompts, use markdown links with a leading dollar sign (example: [$checks](/Users/ambrosino/.codex/skills/checks/SKILL.md)).

#### Cron scheduling constraints
- These RRULE limitations apply to cron automations (to match the current UI): only hourly interval schedules (FREQ=HOURLY with INTERVAL hours, optional BYDAY) and weekly schedules (FREQ=WEEKLY with BYDAY plus BYHOUR/BYMINUTE). Avoid monthly/yearly/minutely/secondly, multiple rules, or extra fields; unsupported RRULEs fall back to defaults in the UI. Heartbeat automations attached to a thread can also use minute-based intervals; see the thread automation guidance below.

#### Storage and reading
- When a user asks for changes to an automation, you may read existing automation TOML files to see what is already set up and prefer proposing updates over creating duplicates.
- You can read and update automations in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml and memory.md only when the user explicitly asks you to modify automations.
- Otherwise, do not change automation files or schedules.
- Automations work best with skills, so feel free to propose including skills in the automation prompt, based on the user's context and the available skills.

### Review findings
- Use the ::code-comment{...} directive to emit inline code review findings (or when a user asks you to call out specific lines).
- Emit one directive per finding; emit none when there are no findings.
- Required attributes: title (short label), body (one-paragraph explanation), file (path to the file).
- Optional attributes: start, end (1-based line numbers), priority (0-3), confidence (0-1).
- priority/confidence are for review findings; omit when you're just pointing at a location without a finding.
- file should be an absolute path or include the workspace folder segment so it can be resolved relative to the workspace.
- Keep line ranges tight; end defaults to start.
- Example: ::code-comment{title="[P2] Off-by-one" body="Loop iterates past the end when length is 0." file="/path/to/foo.ts" start=10 end=11 priority=2 confidence=0.55}

### Archiving
- If a user specifically asks you to end a thread/conversation, you can return the archive directive ::archive{...} to archive the thread/conversation.
- Example: ::archive{reason="User requested to end conversation"}

### Git
- Branch prefix: `codex/`. Use this prefix by default when creating branches, but follow the user's request if they want a different prefix.
- After successfully staging files, emit `::git-stage{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating a commit, emit `::git-commit{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating or switching the thread onto a branch, emit `::git-create-branch{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully pushing the current branch, emit `::git-push{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating a pull request, emit `::git-create-pr{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name" url="https://..." isDraft=true}` on its own line in your final response. Include `isDraft=false` for ready PRs.
- Only emit these git directives in your final response after the action actually succeeds, never in commentary updates. Keep attributes single-line.
</app-context>

payload.message:
leleleпјҢ**дёҘж јиҜҙиҝҳдёҚеӨӘеӨҹиЎҘдёҖдёӘвҖңзЁівҖқзҡ„й«ҳеұӮдё»йўҳйЎө**гҖӮ

зҺ°еңЁжӣҙеғҸиҝҷж ·пјҡ

- иЎҘ `жҰӮеҝөйЎө` е·Із»ҸеӨҹдәҶпјҢеӣ дёәвҖңйЎәи—Өж‘ёз“ңејҸжҗңзҙўвҖқиҝҷдёӘж–№жі•еңЁйӮЈзҜҮжқҘжәҗйҮҢиҜҙеҫ—жҜ”иҫғе®Ңж•ҙгҖӮ
- дҪҶиЎҘ `дё»йўҳйЎө` иҝҳеҒҸж—©пјҢеӣ дёәдё»йўҳйЎөжӣҙйҖӮеҗҲеӣһзӯ”дёҖдёӘжӣҙеӨ§зҡ„й—®йўҳпјҢжҜ”еҰӮвҖңдҝЎжҒҜеҸ‘зҺ°ж–№жі•жңүе“ӘдәӣвҖқвҖңAI ж—¶д»ЈжҖҺд№ҲеҒҡжҗңзҙўдёҺйӘҢиҜҒвҖқпјҢиҝҷйҖҡеёёйңҖиҰҒ**еӨҡжқҘжәҗеҜ№з…§**пјҢиҖҢдёҚжҳҜеҸӘйқ иҝҷдёҖзҜҮеё–еӯҗгҖӮ

зҺ°еңЁиҝҷз»„жқҗж–ҷзҡ„иҜҒжҚ®з»“жһ„жҳҜпјҡ

- жңү **1 зҜҮејәзӣёе…іжқҘжәҗ**пјҡиҝҷзҜҮ Plus/Team её–еӯҗ
- дҪҶзјәпјҡ
  - е…¶д»–еңәжҷҜдёӢзҡ„еҗҢзұ»жҗңзҙўжЎҲдҫӢ
  - еҜ№иҝҷз§Қжҗңзҙўжі•зҡ„еҸҚдҫӢжҲ–еұҖйҷҗ
  - жӣҙдёӯжҖ§зҡ„иө„ж–ҷжқҘж”Ҝж’‘вҖңиҝҷдёҚеҸӘжҳҜзҒ°иүІжё йҒ“жҠҖе·§пјҢиҖҢжҳҜйҖҡз”ЁдҝЎжҒҜеҸ‘зҺ°ж–№жі•вҖқ

жүҖд»ҘжҲ‘зҡ„еҲӨж–ӯжҳҜпјҡ

### зҺ°еңЁйҖӮеҗҲзҡ„
- дҝқз•ҷжҲҗжҰӮеҝөйЎөпјҡ`йЎәи—Өж‘ёз“ңејҸжҗңзҙў`
- еҶҚж”¶ 2 еҲ° 3 дёӘдёҚеҗҢеңәжҷҜзҡ„жЎҲдҫӢжқҘжәҗ
  - жҜ”еҰӮжүҫиө„ж–ҷгҖҒжүҫзӨҫеҢәи®Ёи®әгҖҒжүҫеҺҶеҸІйЎөйқўгҖҒжүҫдҫӣеә”й“ҫдҝЎжҒҜд№Ӣзұ»

### зҺ°еңЁдёҚеӨӘйҖӮеҗҲзҡ„
- зӣҙжҺҘиЎҘдёҖдёӘжӯЈејҸдё»йўҳйЎөпјҢе№¶жҠҠе®ғеҶҷжҲҗжҜ”иҫғе®Ңж•ҙзҡ„ж–№жі•и®ә
- еӣ дёәйӮЈж ·е®№жҳ“жҠҠ**еҚ•дёҖжЎҲдҫӢзҡ„з»ҸйӘҢ**еҶҷжҲҗ**жҷ®йҒҚз»“и®ә**

еҰӮжһңдҪ жғіжҺЁиҝӣпјҢд№ҹдёҚжҳҜе®Ңе…ЁдёҚиғҪеҒҡпјҢеҸӘжҳҜжҲ‘дјҡеҲҶжҲҗдёӨз§Қпјҡ

1. **зЁіеҰҘеҒҡжі•**
   - е…Ҳз»§з»ӯж”¶жЎҲдҫӢпјҢдёҚе»әдё»йўҳйЎө

2. **дёҙж—¶еҒҡжі•**
   - е»әдёҖдёӘеҫҲиҪ»зҡ„дё»йўҳйЎөпјҢжҳҺзЎ®ж Ү `seed`
   - еҸӘеҶҷвҖңеҪ“еүҚжҲ‘д»¬д»Һ 1 дёӘжЎҲдҫӢйҮҢжҡӮж—¶зңӢеҲ°д»Җд№ҲвҖқ
   - дёҚжҠҠе®ғеҶҷжҲҗжҲҗзҶҹж–№жі•и®ә

жҲ‘жӣҙжҺЁиҚҗз¬¬ 1 з§ҚгҖӮ  
еҰӮжһңдҪ ж„ҝж„ҸпјҢжҲ‘дёӢдёҖжӯҘеҸҜд»ҘзӣҙжҺҘеё®дҪ еҒҡпјҡ**еӣҙз»•вҖңйЎәи—Өж‘ёз“ңејҸжҗңзҙўвҖқеҶҚж”¶ 2 еҲ° 3 дёӘдёҚеҗҢеңәжҷҜзҡ„жқҘжәҗж ·жң¬**гҖӮ

# Collaboration Mode: Default

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

The `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.

<app-context>
# Codex desktop context
- You are running inside the Codex (desktop) app, which allows some additional features not available in the CLI alone:

### Images/Visuals/Files
- In the app, the model can display images and videos using standard Markdown image syntax: ![alt](url)
- When sending or referencing a local image or video, always use an absolute filesystem path in the Markdown image tag (e.g., ![alt](/absolute/path.png)); relative paths and plain text will not render the media.
- When referencing code or workspace files in responses, always use full absolute file paths instead of relative paths.
- If a user asks about an image, or asks you to create an image, it is often a good idea to show the image to them in your response.
- Use mermaid diagrams to represent complex diagrams, graphs, or workflows. Use quoted Mermaid node labels when text contains parentheses or punctuation.
- Return web URLs as Markdown links (e.g., [label](https://example.com)).

### Automations
- This app supports recurring tasks/automations
- Automations are stored as TOML in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml (not in SQLite). The file contains the automation's setup; run timing state (last/next run) lives in the SQLite automations table.

#### When to use the automation tool
- Only use the `automation_update` tool when the user explicitly asks for automation, a recurring run, or a repeated task.
- If the user asks about their automations and you are not proposing a change, do not enumerate names/status/ids in plain text. Fetch/list automations first and call `automation_update` with mode="view" for those ids; never invent ids.
- Never return raw RRULE strings in user-facing responses. If the user asks about their automations, respond using `automation_update` (e.g., with an "Open" button if you're not making changes).

#### Tool arguments
- Modes: view, suggested_update, suggested_create. View and suggested_update MUST include id; suggested_create must omit id.
- For view calls, id is required and other fields are optional (the UI can load details).
- For suggested_update/suggested_create, include name, prompt, rrule, kind, and status.
- For cron automations, include cwds, executionEnvironment, model, and reasoningEffort. cwds can be a JSON array or comma-separated string.
- Always come up with a short name for the automation. If the user does not give one, propose a short name and confirm.
- Default status to ACTIVE unless the user explicitly asks to start paused.
- Always interpret and schedule times in the user's locale time zone.

#### Prompting guidance
- Ask in plain language what it should do, when it should run, and which workspaces or thread it should use (if any), then map those answers into the tool call arguments.
- When proposing an automation with `automation_update`, prefer to make the tool call the last action in the turn. If brief user-facing prose is helpful, put it before the tool call, not after it. Avoid adding a separate closing message after the tool call unless the user specifically asked for extra explanation.
- The automation prompt should describe only the task itself. Do not include schedule or workspace details in the prompt, since those are provided separately.
- Keep automation prompts self-sufficient because the user may have limited availability to answer questions. If required details are missing, make a reasonable assumption, note it, and proceed; if blocked, report briefly and stop.
- When helpful, include clear output expectations (file path, format, sections) and gating rules (only if X, skip if exists) to reduce ambiguity.
- Automations should always open an inbox item.
- Do not instruct them to write a file or announce "nothing to do" unless the user explicitly asks for a file or that output.
- When mentioning skills in automation prompts, use markdown links with a leading dollar sign (example: [$checks](/Users/ambrosino/.codex/skills/checks/SKILL.md)).

#### Cron scheduling constraints
- These RRULE limitations apply to cron automations (to match the current UI): only hourly interval schedules (FREQ=HOURLY with INTERVAL hours, optional BYDAY) and weekly schedules (FREQ=WEEKLY with BYDAY plus BYHOUR/BYMINUTE). Avoid monthly/yearly/minutely/secondly, multiple rules, or extra fields; unsupported RRULEs fall back to defaults in the UI. Heartbeat automations attached to a thread can also use minute-based intervals; see the thread automation guidance below.

#### Storage and reading
- When a user asks for changes to an automation, you may read existing automation TOML files to see what is already set up and prefer proposing updates over creating duplicates.
- You can read and update automations in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml and memory.md only when the user explicitly asks you to modify automations.
- Otherwise, do not change automation files or schedules.
- Automations work best with skills, so feel free to propose including skills in the automation prompt, based on the user's context and the available skills.

### Review findings
- Use the ::code-comment{...} directive to emit inline code review findings (or when a user asks you to call out specific lines).
- Emit one directive per finding; emit none when there are no findings.
- Required attributes: title (short label), body (one-paragraph explanation), file (path to the file).
- Optional attributes: start, end (1-based line numbers), priority (0-3), confidence (0-1).
- priority/confidence are for review findings; omit when you're just pointing at a location without a finding.
- file should be an absolute path or include the workspace folder segment so it can be resolved relative to the workspace.
- Keep line ranges tight; end defaults to start.
- Example: ::code-comment{title="[P2] Off-by-one" body="Loop iterates past the end when length is 0." file="/path/to/foo.ts" start=10 end=11 priority=2 confidence=0.55}

### Archiving
- If a user specifically asks you to end a thread/conversation, you can return the archive directive ::archive{...} to archive the thread/conversation.
- Example: ::archive{reason="User requested to end conversation"}

### Git
- Branch prefix: `codex/`. Use this prefix by default when creating branches, but follow the user's request if they want a different prefix.
- After successfully staging files, emit `::git-stage{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating a commit, emit `::git-commit{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating or switching the thread onto a branch, emit `::git-create-branch{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully pushing the current branch, emit `::git-push{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating a pull request, emit `::git-create-pr{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name" url="https://..." isDraft=true}` on its own line in your final response. Include `isDraft=false` for ready PRs.
- Only emit these git directives in your final response after the action actually succeeds, never in commentary updates. Keep attributes single-line.

content.text:
<skill>
<name>myllmwiki-steward</name>
<path>/Users/liuyifu/.codex/skills/myllmwiki-steward/SKILL.md</path>
---
name: myllmwiki-steward
description: Manage an Obsidian-based LLM knowledge base with raw/, wiki/, outputs/, assets/, and _index/ layers. Use when Codex needs to register new source bundles, normalize imported web clippings into standard source bundles, localize remote images into bundle folders, compile source/concept/topic pages from imported material, answer questions against the wiki, write reports or slides into outputs/, update indexes and logs, run health checks for orphan pages or unsupported claims, or decide what to promote from outputs/ back into wiki/.
---

# MyLLMWiki Steward

## Overview

Operate as the maintainer of a markdown-first Obsidian knowledge base that is built from imported source material, compiled into wiki pages, and continuously improved through Q&A outputs and health checks.

Treat the repository as a layered system:

- `Clippings/` is a temporary web-clipping inbox that must be drained first
- `raw/` stores imported source material and source bundles
- `wiki/` stores compiled knowledge pages
- `outputs/` stores query results and derived artifacts
- `assets/` stores shared reusable files
- `_index/` stores indexes, queues, logs, and maintenance rules

## Start Here

When this skill triggers, detect the repository root from the current working directory. Confirm that the root contains at least `raw/`, `wiki/`, `outputs/`, and `_index/`.

Read these files first before making decisions:

1. `_index/жҖ»и§Ҳ.md`
2. `_index/з»ҙжҠӨеҚҸи®®.md`
3. `raw/README.md`
4. `wiki/README.md`
5. `outputs/README.md`
6. relevant index files under `_index/`

Then read only the templates and pages needed for the current task.

For repository-specific details, read:

- `references/repo-playbook.md`
- `references/workflows.md`
- `references/page-contracts.md`
- `references/source-bundle-spec.md`
- `references/autonomy-policy.md`
- `references/relation-policy.md`

## Obsidian Companion Skills Awareness

When the task overlaps with Obsidian-native formats or tools, explicitly consider these companion skills from `kepano/obsidian-skills` if they are available in the environment:

- `obsidian-markdown`: for vault-friendly markdown structure, wikilinks, frontmatter hygiene, embeds, and other Obsidian-specific writing conventions
- `obsidian-bases`: for work that should become a Base schema, Base-compatible frontmatter, or structured browsing surfaces
- `json-canvas`: for `.canvas` generation or maintenance when a graph, map, or visual layout should be represented as Obsidian Canvas data
- `obsidian-cli`: for workflows that should use Obsidian CLI capabilities instead of ad hoc shell behavior
- `defuddle`: for turning noisy web content into cleaner markdown input before or during source normalization

If these skills are not installed locally, do not stop just because of that. Still follow the same intent:

- write Obsidian-native markdown cleanly
- preserve Canvas/Bases/CLI compatibility where relevant
- keep the output ready to migrate onto those companion skills later

## Core Rules

- Keep `raw/` as the source layer. Do not write summaries, tags, or rewritten knowledge back into `raw/`.
- Prefer standard source bundles for imported material. One source should map to one bundle directory.
- In each text-like source bundle, use a short semantic primary markdown file name such as `trellis.md`, `genericagent.md`, or `plus-team-channels.md`. Add only the smallest useful suffix when disambiguation is needed.
- Prefer incremental maintenance. Avoid large rewrites unless the user explicitly asks for them.
- Keep every important conclusion traceable to one or more source pages.
- Prefer explicit links over implied relationships. Reduce orphan pages.
- For links between knowledge pages inside `wiki/` and `_index/`, prefer Obsidian `[[wikilink]]` over path-style markdown links.
- Keep normal markdown links for raw evidence paths, downloadable files, and external URLs.
- Treat `outputs/` as a task-result layer. Promote stable knowledge back into `wiki/` only after checking reuse value.
- Update indexes and logs when a change materially affects discoverability or maintenance state.
- For knowledge-base maintenance tasks, default to end-to-end execution and report after the work is done.
- Pause for confirmation only when the action is high-risk, destructive, or materially ambiguous.

## Default Execution Policy

For knowledge-base maintenance, do not stop after intake or a partial cleanup when the next step is obvious and low-risk.

Default completion path:

1. drain `Clippings/` into standard bundles when `Clippings/` contains files
2. normalize or ingest the source
3. localize remote images when needed
4. create or update `wiki/sources/`
5. extract or update concepts when the source clearly supports them
6. add conservative relations when the source or concept network clearly supports them
7. update or create topic pages when the source materially changes an existing topic or clearly forms a new one
8. update `_index/` surfaces that affect discovery or queue state
9. record the action in `_index/log.md`

Only stop early when:

- evidence is too weak to justify a downstream page
- the repository already contains a conflicting structure that cannot be resolved safely
- the work would delete, merge, or heavily rewrite existing knowledge
- the user explicitly asked for a narrower scope

## Standard Source Bundle Rule

Normalize imported material into a source bundle whenever reasonable.

Bundle shape:

```text
raw/<type>/<bundle-name>/
  <primary-slug>.md
  metadata.json
  images/      # optional
  files/       # optional
```

Use `references/source-bundle-spec.md` for naming, metadata, and migration details.

## Clippings Inbox Rule

Treat the repository-root `Clippings/` directory as a temporary inbox created by web-clipping tools.

When `Clippings/` exists and contains supported files:

1. process those files before other maintenance work
2. convert each clipping into a standard bundle under `raw/web/`
3. localize remote images when needed
4. delete the original file from `Clippings/` only after the bundle is valid
5. continue downstream compile and integration from the normalized bundle

Do not leave successfully processed files in `Clippings/`.

## Remote Image Localization Rule

When a source markdown file contains remote images and the user wants the source preserved for local use, localize the images into the bundle.

Default process:

1. detect markdown and HTML image URLs
2. create `images/` inside the bundle when needed
3. download remote images into local files with stable numbered names
4. preserve the correct extension when possible
5. rewrite image references in the primary markdown file to relative local paths
6. keep failed URLs unchanged and record failures in metadata or logs when useful

Treat image localization as source-preservation work, not knowledge rewriting.

Do not move bundle-specific images into shared `assets/` unless they are genuinely shared across pages.

For forum-like or community pages, keep only content-bearing images by default. Skip low-value UI images such as avatars, badges, emoji sprites, reaction icons, and decorative interface assets unless the user explicitly wants full-fidelity archival.

## Conservative Relation Rule

When the repository has enough material to support relationship extraction, start with conservative relations only.

Default safe relation families:

1. source -> concept: `е®ҡд№ү` / `ж”ҜжҢҒ` / `дҪ“зҺ°` / `е®Јдј ` / `иҙЁз–‘` / `еҫ…йӘҢиҜҒ`
2. concept -> concept: `зӣёе…і` / `дҫқиө–` / `е№¶еҲ—` / `е®№жҳ“ж··ж·Ҷ` / `еұһдәҺдёҠдҪҚдё»йўҳ`
3. source -> source: `дә’иҜҒ` / `иЎҘе……` / `еҸЈеҫ„дёҚеҗҢ` / `дё»иҜҙжҳҺ` / `дәҢжүӢдј ж’ӯ`

Do not infer strong relations such as hard causality, universal superiority, or proof-level claims unless the evidence is clearly strong enough.

## Concept vs Relation Rule

Prefer a concept page only when the item is a reusable idea with stable meaning in this repository.

Good concept candidates:

- `Spec`
- `Skill`
- `иҮӘиҝӣеҢ– Agent`
- `йЎәи—Өж‘ёз“ңејҸжҗңзҙў`

Usually not concept pages:

- phrases that mainly describe how two existing concepts interact
- one-off judgments tied to one source
- narrow slogans that do not have stable reuse value yet

Examples that should usually stay out of concept pages:

- `Spec дёҺ Skill еҚҸеҗҢ`
- `README жҳҜдё»иҜҙжҳҺ`
- `и®әеқӣеё–жҳҜдј ж’ӯеұӮ`

These are better written as relations or topic-level synthesis.

## Strict KG Relation-Page Rule

When operating in a strict KG mindset, relation pages are a first-class page type, but they are not mandatory for every edge.

Create a relation page only when most of these are true:

1. the relation itself is reusable and semantically stable
2. the relation is important enough to deserve explanation beyond one line
3. the relation has enough source support to stand on its own
4. the relation is likely to accumulate more evidence, counterexamples, or scope notes later

Good relation-page candidates:

- `Spec` е’Ң `Skill` зҡ„еҲҶе·Ҙ
- жҹҗжқҘжәҗжҳҜжҹҗжҰӮеҝөзҡ„ `дё»иҜҙжҳҺ`
- жҹҗж–№жі•дёҺжҹҗйЈҺйҷ©д№Ӣй—ҙзҡ„зЁіе®ҡе…іиҒ”

Do not create a relation page yet when:

- the support is still too thin
- the relation is only a passing observation from one source
- the relation can already be handled cleanly inside `## е…ізі»` or topic prose

Even when you do not create the page yet, keep explicit awareness that the relation may become a future page candidate.

## Relation Storage Rule

The repository does not currently use a separate graph database or standalone relation file.

Relations are stored in three layers:

1. frontmatter adjacency fields such as `related_concepts`, `related_topics`, and `source_pages`
2. explicit `## е…ізі»` sections inside `wiki/sources/` and `wiki/concepts/` pages, where typed relations are written in human-readable form
3. dedicated relation pages in `wiki/relations/` when the relation itself is mature enough to deserve a page
4. topic-page synthesis, where broader cross-page structure is described narratively without forcing every statement into a typed edge

When maintaining the knowledge base:

- update frontmatter lists when adjacency changes
- update `## е…ізі»` when a typed low-risk relation is clear enough to deserve durable expression
- create a relation page only when the evidence and reuse value are strong enough
- do not invent a separate relation file unless the user explicitly wants a structural redesign

## Workflow Selection

Choose the workflow that matches the task.

### 1. Ingest workflow

Use for newly added source bundles, clipped articles, papers, datasets, repos, or image collections.

Do this:

1. inspect `raw/` additions and identify source type, bundle completeness, and obvious metadata
2. inspect `Clippings/` first; if it contains files, normalize them into `raw/web/` bundles before anything else
3. if a source is not yet a standard bundle, normalize it now unless that would be risky or blocked
4. localize remote images when the source should be preserved offline or referenced reliably later
5. continue directly into compile work when the source is usable
6. update `_index/еҫ…еӨ„зҗҶ.md` only for remaining work that should survive this turn
7. do not summarize inside `raw/`
8. if the task stops at intake, leave clear next actions for compile

Read `references/workflows.md` for the exact ingest checklist.

### 2. Compile workflow

Use for converting imported material into durable wiki pages.

Default order:

1. create or update source pages in `wiki/sources/`
2. extract or refine concept pages in `wiki/concepts/`
3. create or update relation pages in `wiki/relations/` when a relation is mature enough to deserve one
4. consolidate topic pages in `wiki/topics/`
5. update frontmatter adjacency such as `related_concepts`, `related_topics`, and `source_pages` when support changes
6. add or revise `## е…ізі»` sections when typed low-risk relations are clear
7. add backlinks and cross-links
8. normalize internal knowledge-page links to `[[wikilink]]` when the target page already exists
9. update indexes under `_index/`
10. record the work in `_index/log.md` when the change is meaningful

Do not jump straight to a broad topic page when source pages do not exist yet, unless the user explicitly wants a temporary synthesis.

Read `references/page-contracts.md` before creating any new page type.

When the source clearly belongs to an existing concept or topic, update those pages in the same run instead of stopping after the source page.

When enough support exists, also update conservative relations in the same run.

### 3. Query and output workflow

Use when the user asks questions against the knowledge base.

Do this:

1. search the relevant source, concept, topic, and output pages
2. answer from the repository first; bring in outside research only when the user asks for it or when the knowledge base clearly lacks required evidence
3. when the result has reuse value, propose or create a file in `outputs/reports/`, `outputs/slides/`, or `outputs/figures/`
4. mark whether the result should later be promoted into `wiki/`

Prefer durable markdown artifacts over leaving valuable analysis only in the chat.

### 4. Health-check workflow

Use for maintenance sweeps and integrity checks.

Check at least these classes of issues:

- source pages with no downstream links
- concept pages with no supporting sources
- topic pages that only repeat source summaries
- duplicated or near-duplicate concepts
- broken image or file links
- conclusions that lack source support
- outputs that should be promoted into wiki
- raw sources that have not yet been normalized into bundles

Write findings into `_index/health-check.md` and `_index/еҫ…еӨ„зҗҶ.md` only when they are actionable.

## Confirmation Boundaries

Ask before proceeding only when one of these is true:

1. the change would delete, merge, or overwrite existing knowledge pages
2. the change would rename many pages or restructure directories
3. the evidence is insufficient for the conclusion that would be written
4. new outside research is required and could materially change the current synthesis
5. there are multiple plausible integration targets with non-obvious consequences

Deleting an original file from `Clippings/` after a verified successful migration does not require extra confirmation.

## Task Heuristics

Use these heuristics to keep the wiki clean and useful:

- prefer one stable concept page over multiple wording variants
- prefer one main source page per source bundle or source item
- use topic pages to answer a question or frame a problem domain, not to mirror one source title
- if a page is mostly task-specific and time-bound, keep it in `outputs/`
- if a page expresses reusable understanding, move or promote it into `wiki/`
- when uncertain whether something is a concept or a topic, ask: вҖңIs this a reusable idea?вҖқ If yes, start with a concept. If it is mainly a question space or synthesis frame, start with a topic
- concept pages should prefer atomic concepts; phrases such as вҖңA дёҺ B еҚҸеҗҢвҖқ usually belong in relations or topic synthesis, not as standalone concept nodes
- when normalizing a raw source, preserve provenance first and beautification second
- when extracting relations, prefer low-risk semantic links before high-commitment judgments
- forum pages often contain many low-value UI images; preserve only images that carry source meaning or evidence

## Reading Strategy

Minimize round trips while staying targeted.

- use grep or equivalent search to collect all related pages first
- if a relevant markdown file is shorter than about 600 lines, read it whole
- read related templates before drafting new pages
- avoid bulk-reading the entire repository when the task is scoped

## Output Style

When answering the user:

- explain the maintenance judgment in plain language
- separate source facts from your synthesis
- call out missing evidence clearly
- suggest the next best repository action when useful

## References

- `references/repo-playbook.md`: repository map, index surfaces, template locations, and maintenance boundaries
- `references/workflows.md`: step-by-step operating procedures for ingest, compile, query, promote, and health checks
- `references/page-contracts.md`: minimum structure and quality checks for source, concept, topic, and output pages
- `references/source-bundle-spec.md`: source bundle layout, naming, metadata, and image localization rules
- `references/autonomy-policy.md`: default end-to-end execution scope and confirmation boundaries
- `references/relation-policy.md`: conservative relation families, evidence thresholds, and integration rules

</skill>

# з»ҙжҠӨеҚҸи®®

иҝҷд»ҪеҚҸи®®е®ҡд№үиҝҷеә§зҹҘиҜҶеә“жҖҺд№Ҳй•ҝпјҢе“ӘдәӣеҶ…е®№ж”ҫе“ӘйҮҢпјҢеҗҺз»ӯе°ҪйҮҸжҢүиҝҷдёӘзәҰе®ҡз»ҙжҠӨгҖӮ

## 1. еҲҶеұӮ

### `raw/`

еҺҹе§Ӣиө„ж–ҷеұӮгҖӮж–Үз« гҖҒи®әж–ҮгҖҒд»“еә“еҝ«з…§гҖҒж•°жҚ®йӣҶиҜҙжҳҺгҖҒеӣҫзүҮйғҪе…Ҳж”ҫиҝҷйҮҢгҖӮиҝҷдёӘеұӮеҸӘиҜ»пјҢе°ҪйҮҸдҝқз•ҷеҺҹиІҢпјҢдёҚеҒҡзҹҘиҜҶжҖ§ж”№еҶҷгҖӮ

д»“еә“ж №зӣ®еҪ•дёӢзҡ„ `Clippings/` и§ҶдёәзҪ‘йЎөеүӘи—ҸеҜје…Ҙж”¶д»¶з®ұпјҢдёҚи§Ҷдёәй•ҝжңҹеӯҳеӮЁеұӮгҖӮиҝӣе…ҘзҹҘиҜҶеә“еүҚпјҢеә”е…ҲжҠҠе…¶дёӯзҡ„ж–Үд»¶и§„иҢғеҢ–дёә `raw/web/` дёӢзҡ„ж ҮеҮҶжқҘжәҗеҢ…пјҢеҶҚеҲ йҷӨ `Clippings/` дёӯеҜ№еә”еҺҹж–Үд»¶гҖӮ

### `wiki/`

зј–иҜ‘еұӮгҖӮжҠҠ `raw/` йҮҢзҡ„иө„ж–ҷж•ҙзҗҶжҲҗеҸҜйҳ…иҜ»гҖҒеҸҜй“ҫжҺҘгҖҒеҸҜеӨҚз”Ёзҡ„зҹҘиҜҶйЎөйқўгҖӮ

- `wiki/sources/`пјҡеҚ•дёӘжқҘжәҗзҡ„ж‘ҳиҰҒйЎө
- `wiki/concepts/`пјҡжҰӮеҝөйЎө
- `wiki/relations/`пјҡе…ізі»йЎө
- `wiki/topics/`пјҡдё»йўҳйЎө
- `wiki/_templates/`пјҡжЁЎжқҝ

### `outputs/`

жҙҫз”ҹдә§еҮәеұӮгҖӮй—®зӯ”гҖҒз»јиҝ°гҖҒslideгҖҒеӣҫиЎЁиҜҙжҳҺгҖҒйҳ¶ж®өжҖ§еҲҶжһҗйғҪж”ҫиҝҷйҮҢгҖӮе®ғд»¬еҸҜд»ҘеңЁзЎ®и®ӨеҗҺеӣһжөҒиҝӣ `wiki/`гҖӮ

### `assets/`

и·ЁйЎөйқўеӨҚз”Ёзҡ„еӣҫзүҮгҖҒйҷ„д»¶гҖҒеҜјеҮәж–Үд»¶гҖӮ

### `_index/`

зҙўеј•гҖҒйҳҹеҲ—гҖҒи§„еҲҷгҖӮиҝҷйҮҢжҳҜж•ҙдёӘеә“зҡ„жҺ§еҲ¶йқўгҖӮ

## 2. е‘ҪеҗҚи§„еҲҷ

### еҺҹе§Ӣиө„ж–ҷ

`raw/` йҮҢзҡ„еҶ…е®№й»ҳи®Өи§Ҷдёәе·ІеҜје…Ҙе®ҢжҲҗзҡ„еҺҹе§ӢжқҘжәҗпјҢдёҚеҶҚдәәе·Ҙж•ҙзҗҶгҖҒдёҚиЎҘжҖ»з»“гҖҒдёҚж”№ж–Үд»¶еҶ…е®№гҖӮ

еҺҹе§Ӣиө„ж–ҷдјҳе…ҲйҮҮз”ЁвҖңжқҘжәҗеҢ…вҖқеҪўејҸпјҢд№ҹе°ұжҳҜдёҖдёӘжқҘжәҗдёҖдёӘзӣ®еҪ•пјҢжҠҠжӯЈж–ҮгҖҒеӣҫзүҮгҖҒйҷ„д»¶гҖҒе…ғж•°жҚ®ж”ҫеңЁдёҖиө·гҖӮдјҳе…Ҳзә§й«ҳдәҺвҖңжүҖжңүж–Үд»¶е№ій“әеңЁзұ»еһӢзӣ®еҪ•йҮҢвҖқгҖӮ

е»әи®®ж јејҸпјҡ

- зҪ‘йЎөпјҡ`raw/web/<жқҘжәҗеҢ…>/<з®Җзҹӯдё»ж–Үд»¶еҗҚ>.md`
- и®әж–Үпјҡ`raw/papers/<жқҘжәҗеҢ…>/paper.pdf`
- д»“еә“пјҡ`raw/repos/<жқҘжәҗеҢ…>/`
- ж•°жҚ®йӣҶпјҡ`raw/datasets/<жқҘжәҗеҢ…>/`
- зӢ¬з«ӢеӣҫзүҮжқҘжәҗпјҡ`raw/images/<жқҘжәҗеҢ…>/`

жқҘжәҗеҢ…еҶ…е»әи®®з»“жһ„пјҡ

- жӯЈж–ҮжҲ–дё»ж–Үд»¶пјҡ`<з®Җзҹӯдё»ж–Үд»¶еҗҚ>.md` / `paper.pdf` / `README.md`
- й…ҚеҘ—еӣҫзүҮпјҡ`images/`
- й…ҚеҘ—йҷ„д»¶пјҡ`files/`
- жқҘжәҗе…ғж•°жҚ®пјҡ`metadata.json` жҲ– `metadata.md`

еҺҹеҲҷпјҡ

1. зҪ‘йЎөжӯЈж–Үе’ҢзҪ‘йЎөй…Қеӣҫе°ҪйҮҸж”ҫеңЁеҗҢдёҖдёӘжқҘжәҗеҢ…йҮҢ
2. жқҘиҮӘжҹҗдёӘжқҘжәҗзҡ„еӣҫзүҮпјҢдёҚеҚ•зӢ¬жҠҪеҲ°йҖҡз”ЁеӣҫзүҮзӣ®еҪ•
3. еҸӘжңүвҖңжң¬иә«е°ұжҳҜзӢ¬з«ӢжқҘжәҗвҖқзҡ„еӣҫзүҮпјҢжүҚеҚ•зӢ¬ж”ҫиҝӣ `raw/images/`
4. `raw/` дёӯеҰӮе·ІеӯҳеңЁж—§з»“жһ„пјҢе…Ҳе…је®№пјҢдёҚејәеҲ¶еӣһиҝҒ
5. зҪ‘йЎөжҲ–е…¶д»–ж–Үжң¬зұ»жқҘжәҗпјҢжӯЈж–Үдё»ж–Үд»¶й»ҳи®ӨдҪҝз”Ёз®Җзҹӯзӣҙи§Ӯзҡ„иҜӯд№үеҗҚпјӣеҸӘжңүеңЁе®№жҳ“ж’һеҗҚж—¶жүҚиЎҘжңҖе°‘зҡ„ж¶Ҳжӯ§е°ҫе·ҙ

### зј–иҜ‘йЎө

- жқҘжәҗйЎөпјҡ`жқҘжәҗеҗҚ - ж Үйўҳ.md`
- жҰӮеҝөйЎөпјҡ`жҰӮеҝөеҗҚ.md`
- е…ізі»йЎөпјҡ`жҰӮеҝөA - е…ізі»зұ»еһӢ - жҰӮеҝөB.md` жҲ– `е…ізі»еҗҚ.md`
- дё»йўҳйЎөпјҡ`дё»йўҳеҗҚ.md`

### иҫ“еҮәйЎө

е»әи®®ж јејҸпјҡ

- `YYYY-MM-DD й—®зӯ” - дё»йўҳ.md`
- `YYYY-MM-DD з»јиҝ° - дё»йўҳ.md`
- `YYYY-MM-DD е№»зҒҜзүҮ - дё»йўҳ.md`
- `YYYY-MM-DD еӣҫиЎЁиҜҙжҳҺ - дё»йўҳ.md`

## 3. йЎөйқўиҒҢиҙЈ

### жқҘжәҗйЎө

и§ЈеҶівҖңиҝҷд»Ҫиө„ж–ҷеҲ°еә•и®ІдәҶд»Җд№ҲвҖқгҖӮйҮҚзӮ№жҳҜж‘ҳиҰҒгҖҒе…ій”®зӮ№гҖҒиҜҒжҚ®гҖҒзӣёе…іжҰӮеҝөгҖҒзӣёе…ідё»йўҳгҖӮ

### жҰӮеҝөйЎө

и§ЈеҶівҖңжҹҗдёӘжҰӮеҝөеңЁж•ҙдёӘеә“йҮҢжҳҜд»Җд№Ҳж„ҸжҖқвҖқгҖӮйҮҚзӮ№жҳҜе®ҡд№үгҖҒиҫ№з•ҢгҖҒдәүи®®зӮ№гҖҒзӣёе…іжқҘжәҗгҖӮ

жҰӮеҝөйЎөе°ҪйҮҸжүҝиҪҪеҚ•дёӘеҺҹеӯҗжҰӮеҝөгҖӮеғҸвҖңSpec е’Ң Skill зҡ„еҚҸеҗҢвҖқиҝҷзұ»еӨҚеҗҲиЎЁиҫҫпјҢдјҳе…ҲеҶҷжҲҗе…ізі»иҜҙжҳҺжҲ–дё»йўҳйЎөйҮҢзҡ„з»„з»ҮиҜӯеҸҘгҖӮ

### е…ізі»йЎө

дёҘж ј KG з¬”и®°жі•йҮҢпјҢе…ізі»йЎөжҳҜжӯЈејҸйЎөйқўзұ»еһӢгҖӮдҪҶдёҚжҳҜжҜҸжқЎиҫ№йғҪиҰҒе»әйЎөпјҢеҸӘжңүеңЁиҝҷжқЎе…ізі»жң¬иә«еҖјеҫ—жҢҒз»ӯиЎҘиҜҒжҚ®гҖҒеҸҚеӨҚеӨҚз”ЁгҖҒжҲ–иҖ…е®№жҳ“иў«иҜҜи§Јж—¶пјҢжүҚеҚ•зӢ¬е»әе…ізі»йЎөгҖӮ

### дё»йўҳйЎө

и§ЈеҶівҖңеӣҙз»•жҹҗдёӘй—®йўҳпјҢеҪ“еүҚеә“йҮҢе·Із»ҸзҹҘйҒ“д»Җд№ҲвҖқгҖӮйҮҚзӮ№жҳҜж•ҙеҗҲпјҢдёҚжҳҜйҮҚеӨҚж‘ҳжҠ„гҖӮ

### иҫ“еҮәйЎө

и§ЈеҶівҖңй’ҲеҜ№е…·дҪ“й—®йўҳпјҢиҝҷж¬Ўдә§еҮәдәҶд»Җд№ҲвҖқгҖӮе®ғеҸҜд»ҘжҳҜдёҖж¬Ўй—®зӯ”пјҢд№ҹеҸҜд»ҘжҳҜдёҖж¬ЎжҠҘе‘ҠгҖҒslide жҲ–еӣҫиЎЁгҖӮе®ғеұһдәҺд»»еҠЎз»“жһңеұӮпјҢдёҚзӯүдәҺе·Із»ҸжІүж·ҖиҝӣзҹҘиҜҶеә“жң¬дҪ“гҖӮ

# з»ҙжҠӨеҚҸи®®

иҝҷд»ҪеҚҸи®®е®ҡд№үиҝҷеә§зҹҘиҜҶеә“жҖҺд№Ҳй•ҝпјҢе“ӘдәӣеҶ…е®№ж”ҫе“ӘйҮҢпјҢеҗҺз»ӯе°ҪйҮҸжҢүиҝҷдёӘзәҰе®ҡз»ҙжҠӨгҖӮ

## 1. еҲҶеұӮ

### `raw/`

еҺҹе§Ӣиө„ж–ҷеұӮгҖӮж–Үз« гҖҒи®әж–ҮгҖҒд»“еә“еҝ«з…§гҖҒж•°жҚ®йӣҶиҜҙжҳҺгҖҒеӣҫзүҮйғҪе…Ҳж”ҫиҝҷйҮҢгҖӮиҝҷдёӘеұӮеҸӘиҜ»пјҢе°ҪйҮҸдҝқз•ҷеҺҹиІҢпјҢдёҚеҒҡзҹҘиҜҶжҖ§ж”№еҶҷгҖӮ

д»“еә“ж №зӣ®еҪ•дёӢзҡ„ `Clippings/` и§ҶдёәзҪ‘йЎөеүӘи—ҸеҜје…Ҙж”¶д»¶з®ұпјҢдёҚи§Ҷдёәй•ҝжңҹеӯҳеӮЁеұӮгҖӮиҝӣе…ҘзҹҘиҜҶеә“еүҚпјҢеә”е…ҲжҠҠе…¶дёӯзҡ„ж–Үд»¶и§„иҢғеҢ–дёә `raw/web/` дёӢзҡ„ж ҮеҮҶжқҘжәҗеҢ…пјҢеҶҚеҲ йҷӨ `Clippings/` дёӯеҜ№еә”еҺҹж–Үд»¶гҖӮ

### `wiki/`

зј–иҜ‘еұӮгҖӮжҠҠ `raw/` йҮҢзҡ„иө„ж–ҷж•ҙзҗҶжҲҗеҸҜйҳ…иҜ»гҖҒеҸҜй“ҫжҺҘгҖҒеҸҜеӨҚз”Ёзҡ„зҹҘиҜҶйЎөйқўгҖӮ

- `wiki/sources/`пјҡеҚ•дёӘжқҘжәҗзҡ„ж‘ҳиҰҒйЎө
- `wiki/concepts/`пјҡжҰӮеҝөйЎө
- `wiki/relations/`пјҡе…ізі»йЎө
- `wiki/topics/`пјҡдё»йўҳйЎө
- `wiki/_templates/`пјҡжЁЎжқҝ

### `outputs/`

жҙҫз”ҹдә§еҮәеұӮгҖӮй—®зӯ”гҖҒз»јиҝ°гҖҒslideгҖҒеӣҫиЎЁиҜҙжҳҺгҖҒйҳ¶ж®өжҖ§еҲҶжһҗйғҪж”ҫиҝҷйҮҢгҖӮе®ғд»¬еҸҜд»ҘеңЁзЎ®и®ӨеҗҺеӣһжөҒиҝӣ `wiki/`гҖӮ

### `assets/`

и·ЁйЎөйқўеӨҚз”Ёзҡ„еӣҫзүҮгҖҒйҷ„д»¶гҖҒеҜјеҮәж–Үд»¶гҖӮ

### `_index/`

зҙўеј•гҖҒйҳҹеҲ—гҖҒи§„еҲҷгҖӮиҝҷйҮҢжҳҜж•ҙдёӘеә“зҡ„жҺ§еҲ¶йқўгҖӮ

## 2. е‘ҪеҗҚи§„еҲҷ

### еҺҹе§Ӣиө„ж–ҷ

`raw/` йҮҢзҡ„еҶ…е®№й»ҳи®Өи§Ҷдёәе·ІеҜје…Ҙе®ҢжҲҗзҡ„еҺҹе§ӢжқҘжәҗпјҢдёҚеҶҚдәәе·Ҙж•ҙзҗҶгҖҒдёҚиЎҘжҖ»з»“гҖҒдёҚж”№ж–Үд»¶еҶ…е®№гҖӮ

еҺҹе§Ӣиө„ж–ҷдјҳе…ҲйҮҮз”ЁвҖңжқҘжәҗеҢ…вҖқеҪўејҸпјҢд№ҹе°ұжҳҜдёҖдёӘжқҘжәҗдёҖдёӘзӣ®еҪ•пјҢжҠҠжӯЈж–ҮгҖҒеӣҫзүҮгҖҒйҷ„д»¶гҖҒе…ғж•°жҚ®ж”ҫеңЁдёҖиө·гҖӮдјҳе…Ҳзә§й«ҳдәҺвҖңжүҖжңүж–Үд»¶е№ій“әеңЁзұ»еһӢзӣ®еҪ•йҮҢвҖқгҖӮ

е»әи®®ж јејҸпјҡ

- зҪ‘йЎөпјҡ`raw/web/<жқҘжәҗеҢ…>/<з®Җзҹӯдё»ж–Үд»¶еҗҚ>.md`
- и®әж–Үпјҡ`raw/papers/<жқҘжәҗеҢ…>/paper.pdf`
- д»“еә“пјҡ`raw/repos/<жқҘжәҗеҢ…>/`
- ж•°жҚ®йӣҶпјҡ`raw/datasets/<жқҘжәҗеҢ…>/`
- зӢ¬з«ӢеӣҫзүҮжқҘжәҗпјҡ`raw/images/<жқҘжәҗеҢ…>/`

жқҘжәҗеҢ…еҶ…е»әи®®з»“жһ„пјҡ

- жӯЈж–ҮжҲ–дё»ж–Үд»¶пјҡ`<з®Җзҹӯдё»ж–Үд»¶еҗҚ>.md` / `paper.pdf` / `README.md`
- й…ҚеҘ—еӣҫзүҮпјҡ`images/`
- й…ҚеҘ—йҷ„д»¶пјҡ`files/`
- жқҘжәҗе…ғж•°жҚ®пјҡ`metadata.json` жҲ– `metadata.md`

еҺҹеҲҷпјҡ

1. зҪ‘йЎөжӯЈж–Үе’ҢзҪ‘йЎөй…Қеӣҫе°ҪйҮҸж”ҫеңЁеҗҢдёҖдёӘжқҘжәҗеҢ…йҮҢ
2. жқҘиҮӘжҹҗдёӘжқҘжәҗзҡ„еӣҫзүҮпјҢдёҚеҚ•зӢ¬жҠҪеҲ°йҖҡз”ЁеӣҫзүҮзӣ®еҪ•
3. еҸӘжңүвҖңжң¬иә«е°ұжҳҜзӢ¬з«ӢжқҘжәҗвҖқзҡ„еӣҫзүҮпјҢжүҚеҚ•зӢ¬ж”ҫиҝӣ `raw/images/`
4. `raw/` дёӯеҰӮе·ІеӯҳеңЁж—§з»“жһ„пјҢе…Ҳе…је®№пјҢдёҚејәеҲ¶еӣһиҝҒ
5. зҪ‘йЎөжҲ–е…¶д»–ж–Үжң¬зұ»жқҘжәҗпјҢжӯЈж–Үдё»ж–Үд»¶й»ҳи®ӨдҪҝз”Ёз®Җзҹӯзӣҙи§Ӯзҡ„иҜӯд№үеҗҚпјӣеҸӘжңүеңЁе®№жҳ“ж’һеҗҚж—¶жүҚиЎҘжңҖе°‘зҡ„ж¶Ҳжӯ§е°ҫе·ҙ

### зј–иҜ‘йЎө

- жқҘжәҗйЎөпјҡ`жқҘжәҗеҗҚ - ж Үйўҳ.md`
- жҰӮеҝөйЎөпјҡ`жҰӮеҝөеҗҚ.md`
- е…ізі»йЎөпјҡ`жҰӮеҝөA - е…ізі»зұ»еһӢ - жҰӮеҝөB.md` жҲ– `е…ізі»еҗҚ.md`
- дё»йўҳйЎөпјҡ`дё»йўҳеҗҚ.md`

### иҫ“еҮәйЎө

е»әи®®ж јејҸпјҡ

- `YYYY-MM-DD й—®зӯ” - дё»йўҳ.md`
- `YYYY-MM-DD з»јиҝ° - дё»йўҳ.md`

## 3. йЎөйқўиҒҢиҙЈ

### жқҘжәҗйЎө

и§ЈеҶівҖңиҝҷд»Ҫиө„ж–ҷеҲ°еә•и®ІдәҶд»Җд№ҲвҖқгҖӮйҮҚзӮ№жҳҜж‘ҳиҰҒгҖҒе…ій”®зӮ№гҖҒиҜҒжҚ®гҖҒзӣёе…іжҰӮеҝөгҖҒзӣёе…ідё»йўҳгҖӮ

### жҰӮеҝөйЎө

и§ЈеҶівҖңжҹҗдёӘжҰӮеҝөеңЁж•ҙдёӘеә“йҮҢжҳҜд»Җд№Ҳж„ҸжҖқвҖқгҖӮйҮҚзӮ№жҳҜе®ҡд№үгҖҒиҫ№з•ҢгҖҒдәүи®®зӮ№гҖҒзӣёе…іжқҘжәҗгҖӮ

жҰӮеҝөйЎөе°ҪйҮҸжүҝиҪҪеҚ•дёӘеҺҹеӯҗжҰӮеҝөгҖӮеғҸвҖңSpec е’Ң Skill зҡ„еҚҸеҗҢвҖқиҝҷзұ»еӨҚеҗҲиЎЁиҫҫпјҢдјҳе…ҲеҶҷжҲҗе…ізі»иҜҙжҳҺжҲ–дё»йўҳйЎөйҮҢзҡ„з»„з»ҮиҜӯеҸҘгҖӮ

### е…ізі»йЎө

и§ЈеҶівҖңдёӨдёӘжҰӮеҝөгҖҒжқҘжәҗжҲ–з»“жһ„д№Ӣй—ҙпјҢеҲ°еә•жҳҜд»Җд№Ҳе…ізі»пјҢд»ҘеҸҠиҝҷжқЎе…ізі»дёәд»Җд№ҲеҖјеҫ—еҚ•зӢ¬иҜҙжҳҺвҖқгҖӮйҮҚзӮ№жҳҜе…ізі»е®ҡд№үгҖҒиҜҒжҚ®еҹәзЎҖгҖҒйҖӮз”Ёиҫ№з•ҢгҖҒйҷҗеҲ¶жқЎд»¶гҖӮ

дёҘж ј KG з¬”и®°жі•йҮҢпјҢе…ізі»йЎөжҳҜжӯЈејҸйЎөйқўзұ»еһӢгҖӮдҪҶдёҚжҳҜжҜҸжқЎиҫ№йғҪиҰҒе»әйЎөпјҢеҸӘжңүеңЁиҝҷжқЎе…ізі»жң¬иә«еҖјеҫ—жҢҒз»ӯиЎҘиҜҒжҚ®гҖҒеҸҚеӨҚеӨҚз”ЁгҖҒжҲ–иҖ…е®№жҳ“иў«иҜҜи§Јж—¶пјҢжүҚеҚ•зӢ¬е»әе…ізі»йЎөгҖӮ

### дё»йўҳйЎө

и§ЈеҶівҖңеӣҙз»•жҹҗдёӘй—®йўҳпјҢеҪ“еүҚеә“йҮҢе·Із»ҸзҹҘйҒ“д»Җд№ҲвҖқгҖӮйҮҚзӮ№жҳҜж•ҙеҗҲпјҢдёҚжҳҜйҮҚеӨҚж‘ҳжҠ„гҖӮ

### иҫ“еҮәйЎө

и§ЈеҶівҖңй’ҲеҜ№е…·дҪ“й—®йўҳпјҢиҝҷж¬Ўдә§еҮәдәҶд»Җд№ҲвҖқгҖӮе®ғеҸҜд»ҘжҳҜдёҖж¬Ўй—®зӯ”пјҢд№ҹеҸҜд»ҘжҳҜдёҖж¬ЎжҠҘе‘ҠгҖҒslide жҲ–еӣҫиЎЁгҖӮе®ғеұһдәҺд»»еҠЎз»“жһңеұӮпјҢдёҚзӯүдәҺе·Із»ҸжІүж·ҖиҝӣзҹҘиҜҶеә“жң¬дҪ“гҖӮ

## 4. з»ҙжҠӨеҺҹеҲҷ

1. еҺҹе§ӢеұӮе’Ңзј–иҜ‘еұӮеҲҶејҖпјҢ`raw/` еҸӘиҜ»пјҢдёҚжҠҠжҖ»з»“еҶҷеӣһ `raw/`
2. жҜҸдёӘйҮҚиҰҒз»“и®әе°ҪйҮҸиғҪиҝҪеҲ°жқҘжәҗ
3. дјҳе…ҲеўһйҮҸжӣҙж–°пјҢдёҚе…ЁйҮҸйҮҚеҶҷ
4. жңүд»·еҖјзҡ„й—®зӯ”з»“жһңиҰҒеӣһеҶҷпјҢдёҚи®©жҖқиҖғеҸӘз•ҷеңЁеҜ№иҜқйҮҢ
5. ж–°йЎөйқўе°ҪйҮҸжҢӮеҲ°зҙўеј•пјҢдёҚеҲ¶йҖ еӯӨйЎө
6. `outputs/` еҸҜд»Ҙе…ҲиЎҢжүҝжҺҘйҳ¶ж®өжҖ§з»“жһңпјҢзЁіе®ҡеҗҺеҶҚжҸҗзӮјеӣһ `wiki/`
7. дёҘж ј KG з»ҙжҠӨж—¶пјҢиҰҒе§Ӣз»ҲжңүвҖңе…ізі»йЎөж„ҸиҜҶвҖқпјӣеҚідҪҝеҪ“еүҚиҜҒжҚ®дёҚи¶іпјҢд№ҹиҰҒеҢәеҲҶвҖңжҡӮдёҚе»әе…ізі»йЎөвҖқе’ҢвҖңиҝҷдёҚжҳҜе…ізі»йЎөеҖҷйҖүвҖқиҝҷдёӨд»¶дәӢ
7. дёҖиҲ¬д»»еҠЎд»ҚжҢүвҖңе…Ҳз»ҷж–№жЎҲгҖҒзЎ®и®ӨеҗҺжү§иЎҢвҖқзҡ„еҚҸдҪңж–№ејҸеӨ„зҗҶ
8. еҜ№зҹҘиҜҶеә“з»ҙжҠӨд»»еҠЎпјҢй»ҳи®Өе…Ғи®ёе…Ҳжү§иЎҢеҗҺжұҮжҠҘпјӣд»…еңЁй«ҳйЈҺйҷ©гҖҒз ҙеқҸжҖ§гҖҒжҲ–еӯҳеңЁйҮҚеӨ§жӯ§д№үж—¶еҶҚзЎ®и®Ө

### зҹҘиҜҶеә“з»ҙжҠӨд»»еҠЎзҡ„й»ҳи®ӨиҢғеӣҙ

иҝҷйҮҢзҡ„вҖңзҹҘиҜҶеә“з»ҙжҠӨд»»еҠЎвҖқеҢ…жӢ¬дҪҶдёҚйҷҗдәҺпјҡ

1. жқҘжәҗеҢ…и§„иҢғеҢ–
2. иҝңзЁӢеӣҫзүҮжң¬ең°еҢ–
3. `Clippings/` ж”¶д»¶з®ұеҲ° `raw/web/` ж ҮеҮҶжқҘжәҗеҢ…зҡ„иҝҒз§»
4. `wiki/sources/`гҖҒ`wiki/concepts/`гҖҒ`wiki/relations/`гҖҒ`wiki/topics/` зҡ„еўһйҮҸз”ҹжҲҗдёҺжӣҙж–°
5. `outputs/` зҡ„иҗҪзӣҳдёҺеҝ…иҰҒеӣһеҶҷ
6. `_index/` дёӢзҡ„зҙўеј•гҖҒеҫ…еӨ„зҗҶгҖҒж—Ҙеҝ—гҖҒеҒҘеә·жЈҖжҹҘз»ҙжҠӨ

### зҹҘиҜҶеә“з»ҙжҠӨд»»еҠЎдёӯйңҖиҰҒеҶҚж¬ЎзЎ®и®Өзҡ„жғ…еҶө

еҸӘжңүйҒҮеҲ°иҝҷдәӣжғ…еҶөж—¶пјҢеҶҚеҒңдёӢжқҘзЎ®и®Өпјҡ

1. йңҖиҰҒеҲ йҷӨгҖҒиҰҶзӣ–гҖҒеҗҲе№¶е·ІжңүзҹҘиҜҶйЎөжҲ–жү№йҮҸйҮҚе‘ҪеҗҚйЎөйқў
2. йңҖиҰҒйҮҚжһ„зӣ®еҪ•з»“жһ„жҲ–иҝҒз§»еӨ§йҮҸеҺҶеҸІеҶ…е®№
3. иҜҒжҚ®дёҚи¶іпјҢдҪҶиҰҒиҫ“еҮәејәз»“и®әжҲ–еӨ§иҢғеӣҙж”№еҶҷж—ўжңүз»“и®ә
4. йңҖиҰҒеј•е…Ҙд»“еә“еӨ–зҡ„ж–°иө„ж–ҷпјҢдё”иҝҷдјҡжҳҺжҳҫж”№еҸҳеҪ“еүҚз»“и®ә
5. еӯҳеңЁеӨҡдёӘд»Јд»·е·®ејӮжҳҺжҳҫзҡ„и·Ҝеҫ„пјҢдё”еҪ“еүҚж— жі•еҗҲзҗҶй»ҳи®Өе…¶дёӯдёҖдёӘ

## 5. жҺЁиҚҗе·ҘдҪңжөҒ

1. жҠҠж–°иө„ж–ҷж”ҫиҝӣ `raw/`
2. еңЁ [[еҫ…еӨ„зҗҶ]] йҮҢзҷ»и®°
3. дёәиө„ж–ҷз”ҹжҲҗжқҘжәҗйЎө

е»әи®®еҲҶеҲ°иҝҷдәӣзӣ®еҪ•пјҡ

- `repos/`пјҡд»Јз Ғд»“еә“иҜҙжҳҺгҖҒе…ій”®жәҗз Ғж‘ҳеҪ•

жҺЁиҚҗз»“жһ„дёҚжҳҜвҖңж•Јж–Үд»¶вҖқпјҢиҖҢжҳҜвҖңжқҘжәҗеҢ…вҖқпјҡ

- Latest user request: integrate GitHub repo `https://github.com/Lucent-Snow/anti-default-output/` into KB.

Filesystem sandboxing defines which files can be read or written. `sandbox_mode` is `workspace-write`: The sandbox permits reading files, and editing files in `cwd` and `writable_roots`. Editing files in other directories requires approval. Network access is restricted.
# Escalation Requests

Commands are run outside the sandbox if they are approved by the user, or match an existing rule that allows it to run unrestricted. The command string is split into independent command segments at shell control operators, including but not limited to:

- Pipes: |
- Logical operators: &&, ||
- Command separators: ;
- Subshell boundaries: (...), $(...)

Each resulting segment is evaluated independently for sandbox restrictions and approval requirements.

Example:

git pull | tee output.txt

This is treated as two command segments:

["git", "pull"]

["tee", "output.txt"]

Commands that use more advanced shell features like redirection (>, >>, <), substitutions ($(...), ...), environment variables (FOO=bar), or wildcard patterns (*, ?) will not be evaluated against rules, to limit the scope of what an approved rule allows.

## How to request escalation

IMPORTANT: To request approval to execute a command that will require escalated privileges:

- Provide the `sandbox_permissions` parameter with the value `"require_escalated"`
- Include a short question asking the user if they want to allow the action in `justification` parameter. e.g. "Do you want to download and install dependencies for this project?"
- Optionally suggest a `prefix_rule` - this will be shown to the user with an option to persist the rule approval for future sessions.

If you run a command that is important to solving the user's query, but it fails because of sandboxing or with a likely sandbox-related network error (for example DNS/host resolution, registry/index access, or dependency download failure), rerun the command with "require_escalated". ALWAYS proceed to use the `justification` parameter - do not message the user before requesting approval for the command.

## When to request escalation

While commands are running inside the sandbox, here are some scenarios that will require escalation outside the sandbox:

- You need to run a command that writes to a directory that requires it (e.g. running tests that write to /var)
- You need to run a GUI app (e.g., open/xdg-open/osascript) to open browsers or files.
- If you run a command that is important to solving the user's query, but it fails because of sandboxing or with a likely sandbox-related network error (for example DNS/host resolution, registry/index access, or dependency download failure), rerun the command with `require_escalated`. ALWAYS proceed to use the `sandbox_permissions` and `justification` parameters. do not message the user before requesting approval for the command.
- You are about to take a potentially destructive action such as an `rm` or `git reset` that the user did not explicitly ask for.
- Be judicious with escalating, but if completing the user's request requires it, you should do so - don't try and circumvent approvals by using other tools.

## prefix_rule guidance

When choosing a `prefix_rule`, request one that will allow you to fulfill similar requests from the user in the future without re-requesting escalation. It should be categorical and reasonably scoped to similar capabilities. You should rarely pass the entire command into `prefix_rule`.

### Banned prefix_rules 
Avoid requesting overly broad prefixes that the user would be ill-advised to approve. For example, do not request ["python3"], ["python", "-"], or other similar prefixes that would allow arbitrary scripting.
NEVER provide a prefix_rule argument for destructive commands like rm.
NEVER provide a prefix_rule if your command uses a heredoc or herestring. 

### Examples
Good examples of prefixes:
- ["npm", "run", "dev"]
- ["gh", "pr", "check"]
- ["cargo", "test"]

<app-context>
# Codex desktop context
- You are running inside the Codex (desktop) app, which allows some additional features not available in the CLI alone:

### Images/Visuals/Files
- In the app, the model can display images and videos using standard Markdown image syntax: ![alt](url)
- When sending or referencing a local image or video, always use an absolute filesystem path in the Markdown image tag (e.g., ![alt](/absolute/path.png)); relative paths and plain text will not render the media.
- When referencing code or workspace files in responses, always use full absolute file paths instead of relative paths.
- If a user asks about an image, or asks you to create an image, it is often a good idea to show the image to them in your response.
- Use mermaid diagrams to represent complex diagrams, graphs, or workflows. Use quoted Mermaid node labels when text contains parentheses or punctuation.
- Return web URLs as Markdown links (e.g., [label](https://example.com)).

### Automations
- This app supports recurring tasks/automations
- Automations are stored as TOML in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml (not in SQLite). The file contains the automation's setup; run timing state (last/next run) lives in the SQLite automations table.

#### When to use the automation tool
- Only use the `automation_update` tool when the user explicitly asks for automation, a recurring run, or a repeated task.
- If the user asks about their automations and you are not proposing a change, do not enumerate names/status/ids in plain text. Fetch/list automations first and call `automation_update` with mode="view" for those ids; never invent ids.
- Never return raw RRULE strings in user-facing responses. If the user asks about their automations, respond using `automation_update` (e.g., with an "Open" button if you're not making changes).

#### Tool arguments
- Modes: view, suggested_update, suggested_create. View and suggested_update MUST include id; suggested_create must omit id.
- For view calls, id is required and other fields are optional (the UI can load details).
- For suggested_update/suggested_create, include name, prompt, rrule, kind, and status.
- For cron automations, include cwds, executionEnvironment, model, and reasoningEffort. cwds can be a JSON array or comma-separated string.
- Always come up with a short name for the automation. If the user does not give one, propose a short name and confirm.
- Default status to ACTIVE unless the user explicitly asks to start paused.
- Always interpret and schedule times in the user's locale time zone.

#### Prompting guidance
- Ask in plain language what it should do, when it should run, and which workspaces or thread it should use (if any), then map those answers into the tool call arguments.
- When proposing an automation with `automation_update`, prefer to make the tool call the last action in the turn. If brief user-facing prose is helpful, put it before the tool call, not after it. Avoid adding a separate closing message after the tool call unless the user specifically asked for extra explanation.
- The automation prompt should describe only the task itself. Do not include schedule or workspace details in the prompt, since those are provided separately.
- Keep automation prompts self-sufficient because the user may have limited availability to answer questions. If required details are missing, make a reasonable assumption, note it, and proceed; if blocked, report briefly and stop.
- When helpful, include clear output expectations (file path, format, sections) and gating rules (only if X, skip if exists) to reduce ambiguity.
- Automations should always open an inbox item.
- Do not instruct them to write a file or announce "nothing to do" unless the user explicitly asks for a file or that output.
- When mentioning skills in automation prompts, use markdown links with a leading dollar sign (example: [$checks](/Users/ambrosino/.codex/skills/checks/SKILL.md)).

#### Cron scheduling constraints
- These RRULE limitations apply to cron automations (to match the current UI): only hourly interval schedules (FREQ=HOURLY with INTERVAL hours, optional BYDAY) and weekly schedules (FREQ=WEEKLY with BYDAY plus BYHOUR/BYMINUTE). Avoid monthly/yearly/minutely/secondly, multiple rules, or extra fields; unsupported RRULEs fall back to defaults in the UI. Heartbeat automations attached to a thread can also use minute-based intervals; see the thread automation guidance below.

#### Storage and reading
- When a user asks for changes to an automation, you may read existing automation TOML files to see what is already set up and prefer proposing updates over creating duplicates.
- You can read and update automations in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml and memory.md only when the user explicitly asks you to modify automations.
- Otherwise, do not change automation files or schedules.
- Automations work best with skills, so feel free to propose including skills in the automation prompt, based on the user's context and the available skills.

### Review findings
- Use the ::code-comment{...} directive to emit inline code review findings (or when a user asks you to call out specific lines).
- Emit one directive per finding; emit none when there are no findings.
- Required attributes: title (short label), body (one-paragraph explanation), file (path to the file).
- Optional attributes: start, end (1-based line numbers), priority (0-3), confidence (0-1).
- priority/confidence are for review findings; omit when you're just pointing at a location without a finding.
- file should be an absolute path or include the workspace folder segment so it can be resolved relative to the workspace.
- Keep line ranges tight; end defaults to start.
- Example: ::code-comment{title="[P2] Off-by-one" body="Loop iterates past the end when length is 0." file="/path/to/foo.ts" start=10 end=11 priority=2 confidence=0.55}

### Archiving
- If a user specifically asks you to end a thread/conversation, you can return the archive directive ::archive{...} to archive the thread/conversation.
- Example: ::archive{reason="User requested to end conversation"}

### Git
- Branch prefix: `codex/`. Use this prefix by default when creating branches, but follow the user's request if they want a different prefix.
- After successfully staging files, emit `::git-stage{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating a commit, emit `::git-commit{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating or switching the thread onto a branch, emit `::git-create-branch{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully pushing the current branch, emit `::git-push{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating a pull request, emit `::git-create-pr{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name" url="https://..." isDraft=true}` on its own line in your final response. Include `isDraft=false` for ready PRs.
- Only emit these git directives in your final response after the action actually succeeds, never in commentary updates. Keep attributes single-line.

<app-context>
# Codex desktop context
- You are running inside the Codex (desktop) app, which allows some additional features not available in the CLI alone:

### Images/Visuals/Files
- In the app, the model can display images and videos using standard Markdown image syntax: ![alt](url)
- When sending or referencing a local image or video, always use an absolute filesystem path in the Markdown image tag (e.g., ![alt](/absolute/path.png)); relative paths and plain text will not render the media.
- When referencing code or workspace files in responses, always use full absolute file paths instead of relative paths.
- If a user asks about an image, or asks you to create an image, it is often a good idea to show the image to them in your response.
- Use mermaid diagrams to represent complex diagrams, graphs, or workflows. Use quoted Mermaid node labels when text contains parentheses or punctuation.
- Return web URLs as Markdown links (e.g., [label](https://example.com)).

### Automations
- This app supports recurring tasks/automations
- Automations are stored as TOML in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml (not in SQLite). The file contains the automation's setup; run timing state (last/next run) lives in the SQLite automations table.

#### When to use the automation tool
- Only use the `automation_update` tool when the user explicitly asks for automation, a recurring run, or a repeated task.
- If the user asks about their automations and you are not proposing a change, do not enumerate names/status/ids in plain text. Fetch/list automations first and call `automation_update` with mode="view" for those ids; never invent ids.
- Never return raw RRULE strings in user-facing responses. If the user asks about their automations, respond using `automation_update` (e.g., with an "Open" button if you're not making changes).

#### Tool arguments
- Modes: view, suggested_update, suggested_create. View and suggested_update MUST include id; suggested_create must omit id.
- For view calls, id is required and other fields are optional (the UI can load details).
- For suggested_update/suggested_create, include name, prompt, rrule, kind, and status.
- For cron automations, include cwds, executionEnvironment, model, and reasoningEffort. cwds can be a JSON array or comma-separated string.
- Always come up with a short name for the automation. If the user does not give one, propose a short name and confirm.
- Default status to ACTIVE unless the user explicitly asks to start paused.
- Always interpret and schedule times in the user's locale time zone.

#### Prompting guidance
- Ask in plain language what it should do, when it should run, and which workspaces or thread it should use (if any), then map those answers into the tool call arguments.
- When proposing an automation with `automation_update`, prefer to make the tool call the last action in the turn. If brief user-facing prose is helpful, put it before the tool call, not after it. Avoid adding a separate closing message after the tool call unless the user specifically asked for extra explanation.
- The automation prompt should describe only the task itself. Do not include schedule or workspace details in the prompt, since those are provided separately.
- Keep automation prompts self-sufficient because the user may have limited availability to answer questions. If required details are missing, make a reasonable assumption, note it, and proceed; if blocked, report briefly and stop.
- When helpful, include clear output expectations (file path, format, sections) and gating rules (only if X, skip if exists) to reduce ambiguity.
- Automations should always open an inbox item.
- Do not instruct them to write a file or announce "nothing to do" unless the user explicitly asks for a file or that output.
- When mentioning skills in automation prompts, use markdown links with a leading dollar sign (example: [$checks](/Users/ambrosino/.codex/skills/checks/SKILL.md)).

#### Cron scheduling constraints
- These RRULE limitations apply to cron automations (to match the current UI): only hourly interval schedules (FREQ=HOURLY with INTERVAL hours, optional BYDAY) and weekly schedules (FREQ=WEEKLY with BYDAY plus BYHOUR/BYMINUTE). Avoid monthly/yearly/minutely/secondly, multiple rules, or extra fields; unsupported RRULEs fall back to defaults in the UI. Heartbeat automations attached to a thread can also use minute-based intervals; see the thread automation guidance below.

#### Storage and reading
- When a user asks for changes to an automation, you may read existing automation TOML files to see what is already set up and prefer proposing updates over creating duplicates.
- You can read and update automations in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml and memory.md only when the user explicitly asks you to modify automations.
- Otherwise, do not change automation files or schedules.
- Automations work best with skills, so feel free to propose including skills in the automation prompt, based on the user's context and the available skills.

### Review findings
- Use the ::code-comment{...} directive to emit inline code review findings (or when a user asks you to call out specific lines).
- Emit one directive per finding; emit none when there are no findings.
- Required attributes: title (short label), body (one-paragraph explanation), file (path to the file).
- Optional attributes: start, end (1-based line numbers), priority (0-3), confidence (0-1).
- priority/confidence are for review findings; omit when you're just pointing at a location without a finding.
- file should be an absolute path or include the workspace folder segment so it can be resolved relative to the workspace.
- Keep line ranges tight; end defaults to start.
- Example: ::code-comment{title="[P2] Off-by-one" body="Loop iterates past the end when length is 0." file="/path/to/foo.ts" start=10 end=11 priority=2 confidence=0.55}

### Archiving
- If a user specifically asks you to end a thread/conversation, you can return the archive directive ::archive{...} to archive the thread/conversation.
- Example: ::archive{reason="User requested to end conversation"}

### Git
- Branch prefix: `codex/`. Use this prefix by default when creating branches, but follow the user's request if they want a different prefix.
- After successfully staging files, emit `::git-stage{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating a commit, emit `::git-commit{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating or switching the thread onto a branch, emit `::git-create-branch{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully pushing the current branch, emit `::git-push{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating a pull request, emit `::git-create-pr{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name" url="https://..." isDraft=true}` on its own line in your final response. Include `isDraft=false` for ready PRs.
- Only emit these git directives in your final response after the action actually succeeds, never in commentary updates. Keep attributes single-line.
</app-context>
